listing_name: Cottage! BonPaul + Sharky's Hostel
description:
West Asheville Cottage within walking distance of shops, pubs, bakery, cafes, music, and breweries

The space
Pet friendly private cottage located behind the main house at the Hostel in Historic West Asheville.

We allow one dog for a $25 pet fee. The dog must stay with you at all times and cannot be left alone on the property.

Self check-in starts at 3pm.

Cottage has full size bed, private kitchen and bathroom. Please note also, the shower is not huge. It will fit one person just fine.

All the cool restaurants, pubs and cafe's that West Asheville has within walking distance!

Guest access
Guests have solo access to the cottage, and access to the main house of the hostel.

Other things to note
Parking in our lot is limited. You can also park for free on the street.

listing_name: Tranquil Room & Private Bath
description:
This comfy, peaceful room has off-street parking, wood floors, a comfy queen bed, desk, and closet storage. Ideal for guests who plan to rest here after enjoying Asheville’s vibrant neighborhoods, attractions, and epic mountain trails. Perfect for active, considerate travelers seeking a quiet place to recharge after a day of adventure.

The space
You will stay in a charming brick rancher in desirable West Asheville. The guest room is on the main floor and the private full bathroom is directly across the hallway.

The guest room has wood floors, a large window, a small desk, chair, and a queen-size bed with extra pillows and blankets. The closet has shelves and hangers for storage, and there is a luggage rack to use. 1st floor access with no steps. Linens and towels provided. High speed Wi-Fi is ATT fiber 600mbs.

Features include hardwood floors throughout the main level, granite countertops in the kitchen and baths, a large back deck overlooking the fully fenced-in backyard.

The amenities include: Self check in using a secure lock box for you to arrive at your own convenience. Comfortable QUEEN size bed, a large spa like bathroom with a large soaking tub/shower, various personal sundries, hair dryer, first aid kit and an abundance of plush towels.

While the location is convenient and easy to get to, the neighborhood has a spacious country feel.
• 7 minutes to the heart of West Asheville
• 13 minutes drive to Downtown Asheville
• 12 minutes to New Belgium Brewing
• 14 minutes to The River Arts District

We are a best fit for active, considerate, low maintenance guests, who communicate well through the Airbnb app, and who are looking for a clean, comfortable room to rest in at night in between adventures in Asheville.

Guest access
You will have access to the common areas of the house (living room, kitchen, and dining room) and use of the front porch and spacious back deck. Please keep any cooking to a minimum and clean up after yourself when using the common areas. Laundry room is available with advanced notice (minimum 5 days before check in).

Other things to note
Quiet hours are 10pm-10am.

listing_name: Terrace Cottage
description:
Located in one of Asheville's oldest historic districts, walkable to UNC-Asheville, the Omni Grove Park Inn, restaurants, wine shops, markets, greenways, parks and more! Downtown is walkable on well-lit sidewalks (under 2 miles), a 5-min drive away.

The space
Experience a classic, private, Arts & Crafts-style guesthouse in our beautiful historic district close to the center of Asheville. All linens and other amenities including HDTV are provided so just bring your groceries.

The newly-installed UBIQUITI access point is your reliable connection for online meetings and networking.

The cottage is very private and comfortable, situated in a quiet location in our safe and friendly neighborhood. Nice walk to downtown along tree-lined streets with great markets and restaurants along the way. Walk to the university, botanical gardens, parks and greenways.

Enjoy a real cook's kitchen, tiled bathroom, sunny deck, closet storage, and locking storage for bikes and strollers. Comfortable queen bed in the bedroom and full-size sofa bed in the front room with all linens provided.

Original owners on the property are knowledgeable about area entertainment and recreation opportunities.

A quick twenty minutes drive from our airport with shuttle service available, and the bus line is one block distance running on the hour. Blue Ridge Parkway entrance is a ten minute drive and our celebrated trout streams, hiking trails and the Smokies offer excellent day trips.

Guest access
The cottage and deck, outdoor cooking tools, and our beautiful neighborhood just steps away and walkable/short drive to downtown!

listing_name: Romantic, Moroccan-Influenced Cottage
description:
One of a kind artist owned and designed cottage in heart of East-West Asheville. Walkable to restaurants/shops, 2 miles from downtown and 5 minutes to Biltmore Estate. TWO beds total.

The cottage has a Moroccan vibe and includes handmade pottery, art, and textiles. Walls are clay plaster and all bedding is cotton. Eco friendly cleaning supplies used.

Try the outdoor tub for hot bath also!

Parking is on Bradley street in front of the cottage or main house. Totally private.

The space
Two beds-queen bed upstairs and one single bed above dining nook. Your own cottage ( no shared interior space) in the heart of East West Asheville. Unique two-story cottage built by owner. It looks and feels like a house you may find in Morocco or Majorca . It's only 7 years old , but built to look like an old villa. Heating and air conditioning on both levels, ceiling fan above bed. 100 % cotton bedding and throws are throughout the house. All natural cleaning products, shampoo, and soaps.Previously named The Mermaid Cottage.

5 minutes from Biltmore Estate. Walking distance to Haywood Rd.
Owl Bakery, Hole Donuts, Short Street Cakes, Cooperative Coffee house, Taco Billy and my favorite for dinner two 5 star restaurants: The Admiral or Leo’s are walking distance or a 3 -5 minute drive.

Whist is a very cool paper goods and many cute things store on Haywood a few blocks from the house.

Two outdoor only cats, Rue (gray) and Gingko (black) live on the property and sometimes wander around the cottage door looking for an ear scratch. Cats and dogs live in my house- the blue one.

Guest access
Guest cottage is private with shared firepit. On the property: Guests can enjoy visiting the chickens, petting an outdoor cat or two or giving a belly rub to our Aussie Doodle- Chai. Cats don’t go inside the cottage. ( and should not)

Other things to note
Total of two beds. Biltmore estate is five minutes away by car. The cottage is one of a kind. The clay/plaster walls create a sense of sanctuary, in the style of a Moroccan villa, far from the business of the city. Then, open the door and travel only a few blocks to some of the best restaurants and parks in Asheville.

Refer to \the space\" to find instructions for remotes for heating and a/c
House manual also has these instructions. Coffee and organic creamer available."

listing_name: Cozy Asheville Retreat
description:
Welcome to our charming and cozy Airbnb nestled in a tranquil neighborhood, just 1.5 miles away from the vibrant heart of downtown Asheville. With a king-size bed, infrared sauna, a private driveway, and your very own entrance, this inviting space is perfect for up to four adults, offering you the ideal blend of comfort and convenience.

The space
Welcome to our cozy haven! Our space is designed for your utmost comfort and relaxation. The spacious king-size bed promises a restful night's sleep, and if you have additional guests, there's a pull-out sofa bed with a comfortable foam mattress topper, ensuring a good night's sleep for everyone.

For your culinary delights, the space boasts a full kitchen equipped with all the necessary supplies to cook meals. Whether you're a gourmet chef or just looking to prepare a quick snack, you'll find modern appliances, cookware, and utensils at your disposal.

Entertainment is at your fingertips with a TV equipped with Roku for streaming your favorite shows and an HDMI cable to hook up your personal computer, making it easy to catch up on work or enjoy a movie night.

As a unique feature, we offer an infrared sauna for our guests. After a day of exploring, unwind and relax in our sauna to soothe your muscles and rejuvenate your body. It's the perfect way to enhance your stay and leave you feeling refreshed and revitalized.

Freshen up in the full bathroom featuring a shower (please note that there's no bathtub). We provide plush towels and a few complimentary toiletries to enhance your stay.

You'll also enjoy the convenience of a private driveway that can hold two cars, making parking hassle-free. Please note that this is a basement apartment, and while it is completely private, we do live in the home above. A complimentary coffee bar is available to kickstart your mornings, and we offer convenience drinks and snacks for a small fee to keep your cravings at bay.

We guarantee a clean and sanitized space for you to ensure a safe and comfortable space for all of our guests. As a basement apartment, it's important to note that occasionally, bugs may find their way in. However, please rest assured that we go to great lengths to keep them out and provide a bug-free environment.

Please note that NO SMOKING is permitted anywhere on the property, even outside on the driveway, to ensure a comfortable and smoke-free environment for all guests.

Private Entrance and Driveway:
Enjoy the convenience of your own private entrance and driveway, allowing you to come and go as you please without any hassle. Park your car right at your doorstep, making it easy to load and unload your belongings.

Relaxing Neighborhood:
Nestled in a quiet and friendly neighborhood, our Airbnb offers a peaceful retreat after a day of exploration. Take a leisurely stroll through the neighborhood streets, enjoy the fresh air, and soak in the local ambiance.

Convenient Location:
Despite its tranquility, you're just a short 1.5-mile drive from the bustling downtown area. Explore the city's vibrant dining scene, cultural attractions, and entertainment options with ease. Whether you're here for business or leisure, our location provides easy access to all the city has to offer.

1 mi. to the Historic Grove Park Inn and Spa.
4 mi. from the Biltmore Estate.
2 min walk to the iconic Asheville Pizza and Brewing Company.
1 mi. from UNCA campus.
1 mi. to Blue Ridge Parkway access.

Guest access
During your stay at our cozy basement apartment, you will have exclusive access to the following amenities and areas:

Private Entrance: You'll have your own private entrance, allowing you to come and go as you please without disturbing anyone else.

Living Area: The spacious living area is yours to enjoy, featuring a comfortable sofa and a TV equipped with Roku for streaming your favorite shows. There's also an HDMI cable provided for connecting your personal computer.

Full Kitchen: Our well-equipped kitchen is at your disposal, complete with modern appliances, cookware, and utensils. You can prepare meals, brew coffee, or simply store your favorite snacks and beverages in the fridge.

King Bedroom: The bedroom features a luxurious king-size bed for a restful night's sleep. You'll find ample storage space in the closet and dressers to keep your belongings organized.

Infrared Sauna: Instructions on how to use the sauna are located in the space. Relax and unwind after a long day and give yourself a spa treatment.

Full Bathroom: Freshen up in the full bathroom, which includes a shower (please note that there's no bathtub). We provide plush towels and complimentary toiletries for your convenience.

Private Driveway: Park your car with ease in your own private driveway, which can accommodate two cars, making parking hassle-free.

Coffee Bar: Enjoy a complimentary coffee bar to kickstart your mornings with a freshly brewed cup of coffee or tea.

Convenience Drinks and Snacks: We offer a selection of convenience drinks and snacks for a small fee, so you can satisfy your cravings without leaving the comfort of your temporary home.

We want to ensure that your stay is as comfortable and enjoyable as possible, and we're here to assist you with any questions or special requests you may have. Please don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything during your visit.

Note: For the safety and comfort of all guests, please be aware that SMOKING is STRICTLY PROHIBITED anywhere on the property, including the driveway.

Other things to note
There is no TV service, but there is a screen and roku with hdmi hook up, and WiFi is available. Please no streaming of unauthorized data/movies/shows on our internet account, thank you.

description: Quiet and Convenient"
listing_type:
Very Clean,
Very Private

And Very Zen

The space
You will be staying in peaceful, quiet, light filled private 1 Bedroom Master Suite with QUEEN size bed, large spa like Bath, with a totally private entrance and hallway in the hub of West Asheville. We have also provided you with an off the street private driveway to park your car which is located a few feet from the gate entrance to ZenShe. This guest suite (ZenShe) is in the main house but does NOT visually share any part of the of owners quarters. Home owners enter through their front door and through a vestibule into their home. Airbnb guest enter through the side entrance of the home into a hall way leading to the guest suite. The Airbnb guest suite and the homeowners living space are separated by two sets of French doors. Any sound you might hear are just homeowners coming and going through their vestibule as they enter or leave their home. Our cat's name is Joe. He is a long, very friendly, black pussycat. You might hear us calling for him to come in at the days end.

We are a totally LEGAL AirBnB, so you can book with confidence! PLEASE read our reviews for further confirmation.

The amenities include: Self check in. We have provided you with a secure lock box and a secure PIN number. This affords you to arrive at your own convenience. listing_name: The Nest
description:
The Nest is a cozy home located about 10 mins from downtown Asheville. Come swing on the porch while you enjoy the beautiful mountain views. The home has stylish furniture, stocked kitchen, washer/dryer, fire pit, etc. The kitchen has granite countertops that opens to the dining area. Hardwood floors exist in the common areas and carpets in the bedrooms. The home is not cluttered & makes a perfect get away with mountain views and easy interstate access to all Asheville has to offer.

The space
This beautiful fully furnished home is available year round for Airbnb guests. Beautiful mountain views are seen from all windows. It is located in a newly developed quaint neighborhood just on the east edge of Asheville. It is conveniently located near shopping and restaurants. Downtown Asheville & the Biltmore Estate are about 10-12 mins away.

The home has 3BR, 2.5BA with an open floor plan. The kitchen has granite countertops and stainless appliances. Everything is new including the furniture and washer & dryer. Upstairs, there are 2 bedrooms (each with 2 twin beds). The master is on the main level. The home is approximately 1750 square feet.

The deep front porch boasts a bench swing as well as two lounge chairs and table. It is a great place to hang out after a day of hiking. You can see the tip of Mt. Pisgah (which I think is 20+ miles away) from the porch (and upstairs windows) on clear days.

For EV Charging - The house has a receptacle type NEMA 14-50R. See picture.

The home receives beautiful sunlight throughout the day and is decorated in a nice and comfortable style making it the perfect nest and year round get away!

Guest access
Entire home

Other things to note
There is parking space for 2 cars in the driveway. Overnight street parking is not allowed per the HOA.

listing_name: Chic, spotless - your home in Asheville!
description:
Private home. 3rd BR is a designated work space. Large yard. Also, renters are welcome to enjoy the park-like meadow, fire pit, pavilion, corn hole, boccie ball, creek - across the street where we live. 5 mi from DT. Short drive to the parkway for mountain vistas and hikes or to the River Arts District, music venues... wonderful restaurants, grocery stores, lake and bird sanctuary for perfect size daily walks.

The space
This is a 3 BEDROOM, bath and a half. (Its 3rd BR IS A DESIGNATED WORKSPACE.) The full BATH has a tub shower with new tile, toilet, sink, flooring and shower fixture. the primary bedroom has a half bath, also with a new toilet and tile floor.
I am a clean freak and so it is uncluttered but contains everything you need to feel at home and to enable your return from explorations in and around Asheville to feel comfortable and relaxing. The meadow across the street is yours to enjoy for daily walks along the creek or for rests in the hammocks or use of the fire pit, the small pavilion, a game of cornhole or bocce ball. This is a peaceful country-escape just minutes from everywhere. (see Refocus Cottage for additional photos).
Shop for unique gifts at the FOLK ART CENTER or the RIVER ARTS DISTRICT, enjoy dinner, a play, a concert, laugh on the Comedy Bus tour, sample our micro-breweries on the BREWS CRUISE, bike, enjoy vistas in the BLUE RIDGE MOUNTAINS (access to the PARKWAY just up the road).
Enjoy local restaurants 828 or Madness and Sushi...
The KITCHEN is VERY WELL APPOINTED with a KEURIG coffee maker, GAS stove, stainless fridge, solid maple cabinets and granite counter tops so you DON'T HAVE TO EAT OUT if you don't want to! (Charcoal grill on a deck). Rest in the hammock under the apple tree. Visit the creekside contemplative spot right across the street. See REFOCUS COTTAGE (airbnb 304379). UNIQUE properties. MINUTES TO EVERYTHING. Just 6 miles drive to DOWNTOWN ASHEVILLE and the BILTMORE ESTATE or the FRENCH BROAD RIVER for a float, kayak or fly-fishing excursion. 20 more will get you to our first class ARBORETUM with its extraordinary bonsai garden, miles of hiking trails, special exhibits, cafe and gift shop. ZIP-LINE, ranked #1!
There is no fireplace or Jacuzzi at REFOCUS FIVE or REFOCUS COTTAGE, but the world class spa at the GROVEPARK INN is on this end of town a few minutes away. Their lobby features two of the largest fireplaces to ever warm your toes and a piano bar to warm your heart with views to stir your spirit. They also have a FIRST CLASS, FIVE STAR SPA with hottubs, views, massages...
RECENTLY remodeled. Two BR's have comfortable QUEEN SIZE beds with quality linens. The third BR, used as a workspace has a SINGLE sofa bed All have plenty of natural light and lovely gardens out the windows. There is a common full bath with a tub/shower (rain shower head. The LIVING SPACE is nicely furnished, with a large flat screen HDTV and great wifi.
Local is a relative term here since downtown Asheville is only a few miles away but here in NORTH ASHEVILLE we have a Fresh Market, Harris Teeter and Trader Joe's, several fine restaurant/bars including vegan, Italian, sushi, Gourmet small plates and tapas, Vietnamese... Asheville Brew and View allows eating while watching a movie in a truly local environment. Consistently fresh and creative Zen Sushi and Cecilia's are two of our local favorites.
Although it is hard to beat the wildlife right here on the property, ONLY TWO MILES away is the bird SANCTUARY on BEAVERLAKE. Walk-able around the lake in 45 minutes, with decking for bird watching and lake gazing. It is a much appreciated nearby choice for guests to walk off the dinner or discuss where to eat next.

Guest access
Your back yard and property is quite private, a free standing 1000sf home on a large lot. AND we invite you to share the park-like property across the street (also in airbnb - 304379) RETREAT like with a HUGE fire pit, pavilion, woods, glen, promenade of pine trees, badminton, boccie ball and bean bag toss.

Other things to note
Asheville is lively and fun, clean and colorful. Get-away and feel at home. Make your stay more enjoyable and affordable. Eat out at Asheville's wonderful restaurants when you want to - not because you have to.

listing_name: Hidden treasure near Downtown, UNCA
description:
Guest room with deck and private entrance. Only a 15 minute walk to downtown for fabulous restaurants, bars, boutiques, art galleries and more! Our property boarders the University of North Carolina Asheville campus. Also close to Asheville Botanical Gardens, North Asheville Tailgate Market, Trader Joes, and Whole Foods. Queen bed and bath with large walk in shower. Small area with fridge/freezer, coffee maker, teakettle. We also stock coffee with accompaniments and selection of tea.

The space
Our private guest bedroom is ready for you! You will have a queen size bed and private bath with large walk-in shower, plus a private deck. This guest bedroom has a private entrance and off-street parking. A small refrigerator and coffee maker (coffee, cream, and sugar!) are provided.

We have two acres that includes several garden areas and wooded land. It is an unusual setting so close to town! The guestroom is located on the first floor of a large addition completed just a few years ago and is attached to the original 1890's home.

You will be very close, walking distance to Downtown Asheville and the UNCA campus. There is great strolling in the neighborhood along some of Asheville's growing greenway system. You may also walk to several near-by spots, 5 Points Diner, Greenlife, Trader Joe's, High Five Coffee are all very close. For nightlife, you will be near to all the hub-bub and fun of Downtown Asheville, totally walkable from our home.

We have a small sweet dog and one mellow cat.

If you enjoy gardens, you will like to walk about our space. There is something blooming nearly all year-long as we strive to keep our bee-hives happy! Also, we are half a block away from the Asheville Botanical gardens.

We require a two night minimum.

listing_name: West Asheville - Canie Creek Getaway
description:
Located in one of the oldest neighborhoods in West Asheville. Sidewalks, friendly people, and the Canie Creek Greenway runs adjacent to the backyard. Indie shops, great restaurants, small breweries, and bars are just a mile or 2 away. It's a quick 5 mile drive to downtown Asheville, and about 20 minutes from some great views and hikes in almost every direction.

The space
Down a few outdoor stairs, you'll enter through a private entrance on the side of the house that leads you to a large 1-bedroom, 1 bathroom apartment. This space includes its own kitchenette, dining area, living room, bedroom, and bathroom. We live upstairs and don't share any of this interior space with our guests. The space is equipped with wi-fi, a fridge with a small freezer, microwave, toaster, a standard stove/oven, and other essentials. The side patio outside the door is entirely yours and you're also welcome to read a book and enjoy a drink in one of the hammocks underneath the deck. Guests are also welcome to use the fire pit on the other side of the house with a little advance notice.

Guest access
We have 1 spot for off street parking on the right side of the house by the firepit. Please do not block the driveway if you park here. On street parking is also allowed and free on both sides of the street, but the street is pretty narrow. If you decide to park on the street, please park as close to the curb as possible.

Other things to note
My partner and I live upstairs with our cat. We're generally pretty quiet, but you may occasionally hear us walking around above. The only other shared wall is our downstairs office, which is on other other side of your bathroom.

On weekends especially, you may also see us outside doing yard work or sitting on the back deck. However, we will always try to give you as much privacy as possible.

On very rare occasions, like a power outage or other home emergency, we may need to ask for access to the space in order to get to the fuse box or the furnace, which is only accessible by going through a door in your space. We'll give you as much advance warning as possible if this is necessary.

Asheville homestays (rentals shorter than 1-month) laws are fairly strict within the city limits of Asheville. Owners must live on the premises or the rental is subject to large fines.

description:
• 2 miles N. of downtown, super central!
• Private 600 sq. ft. full-sun downstairs studio
• Private entry, lots of light, CLEAN!
• Bay window area for workspace/dining.
• Wi-Fi
• Your own AC/Heat

• $50 pet fee, add them as 'pet' to your reservation. Dogs ok, no cats

The space
-Sorry, no refund for allergies to cats/dogs.
-Just let me know before booking with pets, $50 pet fee (total).
-At top of the driveway in FRONT of the house is a black trashcan to dispose of puppy poop bags.

Our mid-century ranch is on a wooded private lot and centrally located in the neighborhood of North Asheville, 2 miles and a few minutes drive from downtown.

Our neighborhood is off the main road where you'll find plenty of restaurants, Asheville Brewery (one of oldest) and grocery stores.

2 miles down the road is our eclectic downtown with boutiques, shops, art galleries, coffee shops and too many breweries to count. We have an excellent restaurant and bar scene rivaling any city in the country. There's nearly always street entertainment and seasonal festivals. We're super central to all this just minutes down the road.
Biltmore Estate is an easy 5 more miles south, no expressway travel needed.

From downtown, it's just across the French Broad River to West Asheville and Haywood Rd, the main street filled with restaurants, bars, coffee houses and shops.

We're only a few miles from the River Arts District, Asheville's central location for art galleries and art studios galore! You'll find painting, glass making, ceramic, jewelry, metal work, stained glass, and much more. Plus food, coffee and shops to make it a day.

We live upstairs and are available if you have questions or concerns, or need help with the woodburning stove. You will probably hear us from time to time, we try to be quiet, but live upstairs.

The full-sun basement studio is a spacious 600 sq. ft., comfortable for relaxing and hanging out. Enjoy the living room area in front of the fireplace or TV (we supply the firewood), or the dining area in front of the bay windows. Wi-fi for internet access. Its a lovely, spacious workspace, and the coffee/tea bar is within reach.

We have a super comfy King size bed and a queen size futon as well, if you don't share the bed. Beds and comfort on vacation are priority!

Private bathroom with shower. The kitchenette has a microwave, oversized toaster oven, coffee maker, full size fridge, hotplate and cookware, and a cast iron farm sink. Fully stocked with dishes, etc for simple food prep and eating. Washer/dryer are right past the bathroom. We're less than a mile from the grocery store to stock up on snacks and easy meals.

Hang out on the little patio in front of your entry, very private as well. There's an umbrella table for two right outside the door.

We have wi-fi, not cable tv. There is a TV with an Amazon Fire for streaming, and wifi for your computer. We share our HBO.

Permit Number-15-15558

Guest access
We're on Inglewood Rd. At the bottom of the driveway you'll see 2 mailboxes (ours says #9) and a red house above the driveway. Drive up, there's a Navy blue house on the right (#9), park in front of the deck, there's an AirBnB Parking sign for your space. To get to your suite, follow the path around the back of the deck and down the garden stairs to your private entrance at the french doors. There's a motion sensor light for after dark, but do watch the steps down the path!

Uber and Lyft are popular in our neighborhood and useful for an evening downtown.

Other things to note
There's a futon if guests need separate sleeping.

listing_name: Downtown Artist Loft
description:
If you're looking for a secluded mountain getaway this is not the place but if you are looking to be in the heart of downtown festivities then our loft is the perfect place for you! We are right in the center of all the downtown action. You are literally steps away from incredible craft beers, award-winning food and regional music. Downtown is lively especially at night where you can hang out and see a local band or the drum circle then sleep late then head downstairs for a massage.

The space
Luxury 1 bedroom loft located in the heart of downtown Asheville.
Queen sized bed in bedroom. Couch pulls out to a queen sleeper.
Our loft has local art, full window view of College street, high ceilings and exposed brick. Downtown Asheville is energetic and in the center of the action. Lots of spaces are waiting to be explored, including amazing restaurants and nightlife. Two doors down is the One Stop/Asheville Music Hall, which features funk, reggae, and rock.
The loft is close to several parking decks. You will not need to drive unless you are going to the River Arts District, Biltmore House or hiking in the mountains. Located between Lexington Ave & Haywood St, the loft is a 1 minute walk to Pack Square and Biltmore Ave and a 3 minute walk to the Grove Arcade & Wall St.

listing_name: The Hideaway, W. Asheville.  2 BR Perfect Location
description:
Read our reviews! 2 bedroom house upper level, centrally located in the middle of the bustling Haywood Rd. corridor, on a quiet side street. Park your car in a driveway and forget about it! Everything you could possibly need is a short walk away. Breweries, award winning restaurants, you name it! Enjoy a beer at one of 8 walkable breweries and then come back and chill on the gigantic covered porch. The Hideaway is inspected by the City of Asheville as a LEGAL short term rental.

The space
Private and spacious two bedroom (king/queen), one bath unit on the main level of a house. Includes a gigantic covered porch where you can chill out after a day of adventure. A short walk to award winning restaurants, music, breweries, parks, artist studios, and shops. Registered with and inspected by City of Asheville, we are a LEGAL vacation rental.

Highlights:
Unbeatable location and comfort! Cozy beds, a large, climate controlled living space, huge fully stocked kitchen, dedicated dining area and the largest covered porch on the block will make you feel right at home! Sleeps 4 adults

The Hideaway has outdoor appeal but is only a short walk from virtually every downtown convenience imaginable.

Location is Everything!
The Hideaway is located within ONE mile of... bars (x20 plus), restaurants (x20 plus), breweries (x10 at last count!), stationary shop, print shop, laundromat, nail salon, hair salon, baby store, consignment shop, vintage clothing store, bike shop, tattoo salons (x8), acupuncture, massage school, yoga studio, climbing wall, fitness center, grocer, market, bakeries(x3), dog park, cafe, coffee roaster, music venues (x10), banks, art studios, mechanic, pawn shop, hardware store...You name it! Its here!

We provide, complimentary, local roasted coffee, tea, cream and sugar. Also, salt, pepper, balsamic vinegar and olive oil. White noise machines in bedrooms.

Important note: The Hideaway is registered, permitted, and inspected by the City of Asheville. Unregistered vacation rentals are illegal in the City of Asheville. Be aware that if you book an unregistered rental, you run the risk of having your booking cancelled if the owner is cited by the city. Book wisely!

Check in at 3 PM. Check out at 11 AM.

We are available 24/7 to facilitate your needs. Please let us know how we can help with tours, reservations, recommendations, and directions for the area. Contact us for more info.

Asheville is a small city filled with folks who think in all directions, like to have fun, appreciate art and craftsmanship, and don't mind hard work. These things are evident in the eclectic nature and variety of businesses, shops, and menus one will find in every corner of the city. You could take a class on mushroom cultivation, permaculture, yoga, tai chi, print art, or brewing. Or join a tour! There is guided mountain biking, guided art strolls, historical tours, comedy tours, haunted tours, haunted comedy tours, pedal powered trolley brew tours, and running or cycling- brew tours. It is Beer City USA after all!

Contact us for more info on any of the above!

If your flying in, the Hideaway is 12 miles from Asheville Regional Airport. Once your here, It's an easy walk to any West Asheville business or park. The eclectic River Arts District (RAD) is just a mile away! Downtown historic district (Asheville proper) is approximately 2 miles away. River access is less than one mile away at Carrier Park. Here, you can put in a canoe, kayak or tube and float the river. The park also has many miles of walking trails, a dog park, a velodrome, volleyball, baseball and soccer fields, and a field hockey rink.
The Hideaway is cleaned with non-toxic products! In addition to a thorough cleaning, all bed linens are laundered and light switches, handles, and remotes are thoroughly sanitized before each guest visit.

Guest access
Entire upper level of house, gigantic covered porch, back yard, fire pit, and grill.

Other things to note
Sorry but we are unable to accommodate pets of any kind at this time.

listing_name: Sweet Sanctuary on Chicken Hill, near downtown
description:
Welcome to my stylish and comfy River Arts Cottage… the perfect home base for your adventures in Asheville! This 2 BR, 1.5 BA single-family home is right in the heart of Asheville with all of its restaurants, shops, galleries, and general awesomeness! This modern yet cozy home is fully furnished, including all linens, towels, soaps, etc. Also included are ALL utilities, even the lightning-fast AT&T fiber Internet, which is perfect for working from home.

The space
Located in the hip and trendy River Arts District, your Asheville abode offers a large front porch with beautiful sunset views and a fun, private spot to enjoy both the beautiful springtime weather and the mountain and river views.

Inside, you’ll find a fully outfitted kitchen with full size fridge with ice-maker, full size dishwasher, gas range, microwave, coffee maker, high-quality pots and pans, colorful Fiestaware, and lots of thoughtful extras like a blender, cutting boards, spatulas, bottle openers, etc. The kitchen also has a pantry with lots of shelves, as well as spice racks for all your spices. There are plenty of kitchen tools for any meal you might plan!

The living room decor is inviting, cozy, and comfy. The wood-beamed ceiling offers a rustic farmhouse vibe and you’ll find plenty of interesting memorabilia, books, and area info to pore over. The TV is Roku-enabled with various streaming apps including Amazon and Netflix, as is the 2nd TV in the master bedroom.

The convenient half-bath is located just beyond the kitchen. There is also a large storage closet on the first floor which is perfect for storing bikes, suitcases, and other items.

Upstairs are two comfortable and spacious Queen bedrooms, each with vaulted ceilings, ceiling fans, generously sized closets, luggage racks, and extra pillows and blankets.

The large full bath features a deep tub, perfect for soaking, and tiled walls. The full-size washer/dryer are stacked.

Private driveway with off-street parking for 2-3 cars.

The terraced yard has been lovingly planted with a cherry tree, a quince tree, and several mature silver maples. The property also has several raised beds planted with flowers, herbs, and vegetables available for your use and enjoyment throughout the year.

Guest access
Guests have access to the full house and garden, and free off-street parking for 2-3 cars in the private driveway.

Other things to note
PET POLICY: All pets will be considered upon request. Please let me know if you will be bringing a pet as a pet fee may apply.

description:
Comfortable guest bedroom in our mountain home sleeps two in a queen bed. We have lowest cleaning fee in town, and private, not shared bathroom. This room in our house is the Blue Ridge Room. Private bathroom not shared with anyone, porch view, close to Biltmore & downtown Asheville. Room also has mini-fridge, TV with Roku, & electric kettle!
Our check in time of 5pm and check out at 11 am are firm to allowing cleaning staff to do their jobs and keep cleaning fees low.

The space
We have keypad-operated deadbolt locks on our entry doors for easy check-in! Our home is in an awesome, scenic, and convenient location: we are a short 15-minute drive from downtown Asheville, 10 minutes from the airport, 10 minutes from the Biltmore House, 5 minutes from the Blue Ridge Parkway, and 5 minutes from Interstate 26.

Guest access
This rental includes use of the guest bedroom and bathroom only. Use of the front porch is also permitted when we are not using it. Because this is our private residence, access to all other parts of our house including the kitchen and living room is not permitted. Please be respectful of our private space.

Check in is 5pm. And check out is 11am. We keep our cleaning fees low by cleaning the rooms after working our regular jobs. So we are unable to offer early check in for any reason. This is a budget room, a clean, basic place to sleep. Our house was built in 1996, so it looks like a house built in 1996. It is our home and we hope those who come to share it with us will be respectful guests.

Access to inside the house is through the front door--come and go as you please, after 5:00pm. No curfew--don't be afraid to come in late. No guests over who are not listed on the reservation.

Other things to note
Also included in the room: Mini-Fridge, Electric kettle, TV with Roku to watch your own Netflix or Prime)

We only have parking for ONE VEHICLE. Guests may park a 2nd vehicle overnight nearby at a well-lit shopping center, or 350 yards down the road along the side of the street.

The space

Our finished basement is very basic but just what a traveler needs seeking a shower and soft and clean place to sleep. Basement isn't in the ground, but rather it is the 1st story of our home. This room has a queen bed, couch, desk with chair, mini-fridge, microwave and a TV with a Roku to watch YOUR OWN Netflix or other streaming accounts.

It’s a basement, so if you think you’ll be cold or are sensitive to laundry detergent smells please plan to bring your own sheets/blankets.

Our home is in an awesome, scenic, and convenient location: we are a short 15-minute drive from downtown Asheville, 10 minutes from the airport, 10 minutes from the Biltmore House, 5 minutes from the Blue Ridge Parkway, and 5 minutes from Interstate 26.

You can't beat this deal!

Guest access
Basement only; Includes private bathroom with shower, bed, couch, desk, dresser, and covered private porch/deck. Private access from ground level--no access to upstairs/kitchen/garage. Come and go as you please. No guests over please. No curfew--don't be afraid to come in late as long as you are quiet and courteous.

Other things to note
This room includes parking for ONLY ONE VEHICLE on the street in front of our home.

Our home is not set up to accommodate people with mobility issues, as we are on an incline and there are stairs to the property.

We cannot provide a refund for inclement weather or adverse travel conditions affecting driving or parking access in our neighborhood or the region. Our refund policy is set by AirBnB.

listing_name: Cheerful Home In Historic Downtown Asheville
description:
***2024 upgraded bathroom shower and AC***

This City of Asheville HomeStay approved apartment is situated on a pleasant wooded lot in a beautiful historic Asheville neighborhood conveniently located near downtown shops and eateries.

Great apartment for families, friends, or couples alike. Best suited for 4 guests or less.

The space
Guests will never lack something nearby to do, see, taste, experience! This apartment is conveniently located a mere mile or two of many music venues (such as The Grey Eagle, The Orange Peel, Harrahs Cherokee Center, Thomas Wolfe Auditorium, ...) restaurants (such as Nine Mile, Tall John's, Curate, 12 Bones Smokehouse, Tupelo Honey, Mela...) as well as great places to discover art (such as the Diana Wortham Theatre, Asheville Art Museum, The Blue Spiral Gallery, Thomas Wolfe Memorial, Malaprops Bookstore, Fine Arts Theatre...)

Guest access
-PARKING (space for one vehicle, a second parking space may be possible- guests are asked to communicate their need for a second parking space)
The apartment is located at the rear of the home- a nice feature providing privacy to guests. Upon arrival- facing the home- there is a driveway to the right of the residence that leads to the apartment. You may opt to park in front of the house and walk down the driveway to the apt. If you aren't concerned with backing in or out of the longish driveway, you may opt to drive down to find the apartment's parking pad near the apartment door.

-Wifi Internet- updated for higher speed/stronger signal (plus Smart TV with Apple TV and Amazon Prime streaming) NB: There is no cable television, but guests may sign in to their own streaming options.

-Spacious kitchen area in compliance with City of Asheville Homestay rules - refrigerator, Breville toaster oven, induction cooking burners, microwave, coffee maker, kettle, blender, light provisions such as tea, coffee, sugar, some spices

-Asheville/WNC guide books

-Variety of books by North Carolina writers as well as a number of children/ young adult books

-Natural cleaning products used

-100% cotton towels and sheets

-Tiled bathroom

-Sound sleep machine w/ speaker (plug in capacity for guest Ipods)

-TV (dvd player/no cable service- however there is access to Netflix, Hulu Streaming, Youtube, etc. as it is a Smart TV)

-Assortment of DVD films

-Games (Asheville Monopoly, UNO, Sorry...)

-For guests with small children, there is a BABYBJORN Travel Crib Light, a high chair and a Maclaren umbrella stroller available for use upon request.

-Tennis rackets (2) / basketball (there are courts- tennis/pickle ball and basketball- at the nearby Montford Park.)

-YARD
The apartment faces a large wooded yard. There are chickens in a fenced area in the yard. The chicken fence has an electric charge to keep them safe from predators.

NB: Guests are asked not to use the trampoline out of concern for safety.

Other things to note
***Newly upgraded bathroom shower and AC*** Jan.2024

The house is an early 1900's arts & craft bungalow that was originally a single family home but has been converted to three completely separate apartments- each with its own private entry.

listing_name: Convenient Log Home: View, Hot Tub, Water Feature!
description:
Relax in a peaceful log cabin just 4 miles from downtown Asheville. Enjoy nearby hiking, rafting, disc golf, breweries, shopping, and restaurants. West Asheville is only 8 minutes away, and the River Arts District just 5 minutes. Visit the Biltmore Estate in 15–20 minutes. This cozy retreat offers the perfect balance of mountain serenity and easy access to Asheville’s vibrant culture and outdoor adventures.

The space
Enjoy the best of both worlds—peaceful mountain seclusion and easy access to everything Asheville has to offer. Our warm and welcoming log home is just 4 miles (an 8-minute drive) from both downtown Asheville and the vibrant West Asheville neighborhood. Although we’re close to the city, the property offers a serene, retreat-like setting surrounded by nature.

Spacious & Comfortable Accommodations

Our home features 3 bedrooms and 2 full bathrooms, comfortably accommodating up to six guests (including children). Please note: the home is reserved for registered guests only; outside visitors are not permitted without prior approval.

Downstairs Larger Bedroom:
Features a queen-size Serta pillowtop mattress, large-screen TV, a futon sofa (bring your own linens), and private access to the patio and hot tub.

Downstairs Smaller Bedroom:
Offers a queen-size hybrid mattress and direct access to the main bathroom.

Upstairs Bedroom:
A private retreat with a queen hybrid mattress, its own full bathroom, and serene views of the pond and waterfall.

Upstairs Loft Play Area

A perfect space for supervised children to play. This open loft includes books, a train set, toys, and tents for imaginative fun. We kindly ask that toys remain in the room and are returned to their place after use.

Relaxing Living Room with Streaming Entertainment

Unwind with a Roku 3 player and access to Netflix, Amazon Prime, Disney+, and many other streaming channels.

Fully Equipped Kitchen

Cook up delicious meals in our spacious kitchen stocked with everything you need—refrigerator with water/ice dispenser, dishwasher, glass top stove, oven, microwave, convection oven, toaster, and coffee maker (with coffee provided). You'll also find plenty of cookware, dishes, utensils, baking supplies, plus herbs, spices, and oils.

Note: The gas fireplace is decorative only and not in use.

Inviting Outdoor Spaces

Front Deck (Covered): Features a dining table for 6 and two rocking chairs—perfect for meals with a view or relaxing in the shade.

Back Deck: Includes a shaded pergola with comfortable lounge seating, a dining table with umbrella, and scenic views of the pond and waterfall. It’s an ideal spot to enjoy the sounds of frogs and nighttime nature.

Private Hot Tub & Fire Pit

Soak under the stars in your private hot tub or gather around the fire pit for s’mores, stories, and a peaceful evening beside the soothing sounds of the waterfall. Don’t forget to bring your own firewood!

Guest access
Guests will have full access to the entire home, including the serene front and back decks, a relaxing hot tub, and a cozy outdoor firepit. Please note that the gas fireplace is not available for use.

Other things to note
Important Notes for Your Stay

Security Camera: For the safety of our guests and the property, a single security camera is installed on the exterior of the house. It is positioned to monitor the driveway and parked vehicles. There are no interior cameras.

Septic System: Please note that the home operates on a septic system. To maintain its proper function, we kindly ask all guests to be mindful of water usage and to avoid flushing anything other than toilet paper.

Guest Policy: The maximum occupancy for the cabin is 6 guests, including children. Outside visitors are not permitted without prior approval. This helps us maintain a safe and comfortable environment for everyone.

listing_name: Private Cottage-2 miles North of downtown Aville
description:
This cozy cottage is on a quiet hillside, 2 miles N. of downtown. Enjoy the privacy of your own space: bedroom, living room, kitchen, bathroom, entrance, deck & parking. Walk to hiking trails, downtown, gardens, UNCA, bars, restaurants & a breathtaking sunset/mountain views.
Our whole house water filter system provides glorious chlorine free water for drinking & showering. It's quiet here. A great place to relax. Enjoy a private deck for dining, star gazing or just enjoying the stellar view.

The space
Best of both worlds...Nestled on a private dead end mountain road, yet only 2 miles from downtown...
Experience Asheville's natural beauty by day and it's hopping nightlife easily from our beautiful home retreat.
The cottage is attached to our larger home, but has its own private entrance, balcony, bedroom, bathroom, full kitchen and living room, as well as private off road parking for multiple vehicles.
There is a comfy queen size bed in the bedroom and two fold-out futons in the living room. The futons are best for singles or kids, so the cottage can house 3 or 4 comfortably. Kids and infants are welcome.
This location is perfect for nature lovers, as we are nestled in a large tree canopy. Bears, turkeys and a plethora of birds are often spotted on the property. There are hiking and biking trails with city views within walking distance as well as easy access to the greenway, the botanical gardens, the farmers market, and UNCA. Both Trader Joe’s and Whole Foods are just a mile away.
You are most welcome to use the treehouse in the backyard and the fire pit. Just be aware that these are shared areas and there are rules around using the fire pit. Please ask before using it.
Well behaved dogs are welcome for an additional $20 per stay. We do have a second air BnB below the cottage, so we can not accept dogs that bark often.
This is a legally permitted 'Homestay' by the city of Asheville. Our permit # can be found in the pictures.

Guest access
You will have access to your own space, including a kitchen with a full size fridge/freezer, oven, stove, dishwasher, etc. There is a tv/dvd (with Amazon Firestick Apps incl. Netflix), free and fast wireless internet access as well as access to a private deck overlooking a meditation garden.

Other things to note
The cottage was recently updated with two brand new Mini-Split Heating/Cooling units, so you can control your climate exactly as you like.

Just a 5 minute drive to downtown Asheville. Please note in the pictures that you must climb several steps to your deck and entrance.

The space
The fully furnished suite offers a super comfortable queen size bed, your own private bath, a comfortable sitting area for coffee and meals, and a lovely deck all to yourself. This new addition to my home offers warm, hand raked birch wood floors in the bedroom and lovely tile in the bath. A mini-fridge, microwave and electric tea kettle make you fully self-sufficient for a short stay, but kitchen privileges are possible as well if arranged in advance of your stay. Many wonderful restaurants and bars are walkable, and your stay is just a 5 minute drive from the River Arts District and downtown.

Guest access

During your stay
I am a working artist in a town filled with great art, food, music, and outdoor activities. I will be pleased to answer any questions you might have and help you discover all Asheville and W. Asheville have to offer.

listing_name: Cozy Charm Home only 5 mins to Biltmore & Parkway
description:
Welcome, y’all! This entire home is cozy and private and just 5 minutes to Biltmore Estate & the Blue Ridge Parkway and 10 minutes to downtown Asheville. With 3 bedrooms and 1 full bath, the house sleeps up to 6 guests. Come enjoy!

The space
This listing is for the entire house all to yourself!

My husband and I want a beautiful and restful space when we come home, so we have designed it that way for you, too. The home is unique because while it is simply furnished, it is comfortable, clean, chic, and a happy place to be.

The house has 3 bedrooms & 1 full bath and sleeps up to 6. Wifi, Smart TVs, comfy beds, and a full kitchen. Enjoy the peaceful setting and open space in the living room. There is bar seating that looks into the kitchen so you can talk with your sweetie while making coffee in the morning.

There is a small backyard with a vintage table and chairs for watching the birds or kids play. Come spend time at our Biltmore abode and experience awesome Asheville! All are welcome here.

Asheville is called the Paris of the South because it has tons of local flair, including art, music every night of the week, and incredible dining and drinking. There are James Beard award-winning chefs with restaurants within a block of each other!

Guest access
The home includes wifi, bedding, towels and soap/shampoo/conditioner for showering. It has heat and A/C and is adjustable for your comfort. Coffee & tea is provided as well as a microwave and toaster in the kitchen. There is a small TV with ROKU and a DVD player. I have a few DVDs and you are welcome to bring any you like, though! There is parking available for 3 vehicles.

Other things to note
Please note that there are a few steps up to the back porch, where we enter/exit the home.

If you have any special requests, please let us know and we will do our best to help out!

description:
Our beautiful home is on a quiet hillside, 2 miles N. of downtown. Enjoy the privacy of your own suite with a large bedroom, a private bathroom, entrance & mini kitchen. Walk to downtown, hiking trails, botanical gardens, UNCA, a local Saturday farmers market, brewery, eateries & a breathtaking sunset/mtn view.
Our whole house water filter system provides glorious chlorine free water for drinking & showering. It's a 20 min walk to town, find hikes right out our door.

The space
The space is private, clean and simple. It is an extra large room with a comfortable queen size bed as well as a fold out futon couch. There is a work desk, a breakfast nook, two end tables, a large closet to enjoy. There is also a HD TV with Roku, including Netflix. In addition, there is a small kitchenette with a mini fridge, freezer, microwave, toaster oven. You can also access our washer and dryer.
You will have a private entrance, a private bathroom and private bedroom.
This is more like your own apartment then just a room.
There is a new mini split heating/ac unit, so you have full control of the climate.
Private off-street parking for 1 vehicle.
This location is perfect for nature lovers, as we are nestled in a large tree canopy. Bears, turkeys and a plethora of birds are often spotted on the property. There are hiking and biking trails with city views within walking distance as well as easy access to the greenway, the botanical gardens, the farmers market, and UNCA. Both Trader Joe’s and Whole Foods are just a mile away.

Guest access
You will have access to your own personal fridge, hot water heater, tea and coffee.
You will have your own entrance, so you can be as private as you like with no time restrictions. There is free wireless access, as well as a free shared laundry machine and dryer that you are welcome to use.
Feel free to use the property, including the front gardens and sitting bench. We only ask you to avoid using the top deck without permission, as that space is a separate Air Bnb.
There is a communal fire pit on the south side of the property that you may use with permission. There are a few safety rules, so please check in with me first.

Other things to note
Please be aware that this room is on our ground floor. There are areas of the ceiling that only have a 7 foot clearance, so if you are taller than that, this may not work for you.

There is a second Air BnB above you so there is potential to hear some noise from above. I do ask all my guests to be mindful of the guests below and to observe quiet hours at night.

We are a legally permitted 'Homestay'. This means that the city of Asheville has inspected and approved this room. The permit can be viewed in the pictures.

description:
Cozy Asheville log cabin retreat—ideal for remote work or relaxed retirement living. Enjoy peaceful mountain surroundings, modern comforts, and big savings on extended stays. This rustic cabin blends Southern hospitality with a quiet, inspiring setting perfect for focused work or slowing down. Loved by log cabin fans and rated ★★★★★, it’s a welcoming home base to explore Asheville’s trails, arts, dining, and scenic drives at your own pace.

The space
Extended Stay Savings & Seasonal Charm

BIG savings for extended stays (90+ days)! North Carolina occupancy tax does not apply to stays of 90 consecutive days or more, saving long-term guests hundreds of dollars.

Feel the crisp, refreshing air of fall in Asheville, where cool mornings inspire exploration and adventure. Stroll trails, enjoy local music, or sip craft drinks in one of America’s most vibrant small cities.

Work from Asheville: Our cabin is perfect for remote professionals seeking focus, comfort, and mountain tranquility while staying connected.

Part of the joy of traveling is experiencing places and customs very different than what we’re used to. Because truly, once discovered is never forgotten Asheville, North Carolina is home to some breathtaking places. Asheville has grown into one of America’s best drinking cities and one of the best cities to enjoy live music.

Work from Asheville- professionals who are working remotely to work from here instead.

Nestled in the Blue Ridge Mountains, Asheville enjoys four distinct seasons.
The summer heat is tempered by the elevation and you have gorgeous foliage in fall. Outdoor pursuits abound from hiking and biking to kayaking and the locals have really embraced their mountain town roots.

CHANGE YOUR VIEW FOR WORK or RETIREMENT

The spectacular scenery of the Blue Ridge Mountains is enough to attract many retirees to Asheville, but it also has a lively cultural and creative scene, with art galleries, museums, studios, and the River Arts District. Outdoorsy types will find many opportunities for hiking, mountain biking, fishing, kayaking, and simply enjoying the clean mountain air. Craft breweries, restaurants, and a range of places to live are draws, and North Carolina does not tax social security income.

Spring is a wonderful time in western North Carolina, with trees and flowers in bloom, the Blue Ridge Parkway a bit less crowded, and the waterfalls flowing. The weather is ideal for a hike along one of many trails near Asheville.

Fall Color Map & Fall Leaves Schedule

Fall color in the Asheville, N.C. area generally begins in late September, with the highest elevations (above 6,000 feet) seeing the change first. During the next six weeks, fall color makes its way down the mountainsides into the valleys and foothills. So, no matter when you visit from late September to early November, there will be an area in or near Asheville experiencing its brightest fall color of the season.

*September 29 through October 9
The first signs of fall begin to appear at the highest elevations (above 6,000 feet). Some of these places turn before others (Graveyard Fields/Craggy Gardens). Places to visit at this time:

Graveyard Fields: While not at a 6,000-foot elevation, Graveyard Fields along the Blue Ridge Parkway traditionally is one of the first areas to see fall color because of the particular plant species found there. Take a hike here to see multiple waterfalls framed in fall color.

Grandfather Mountain: Early leaves are also on display at Grandfather Mountain which includes the famous Mile High Swinging Bridge and several favorite hiking areas. (In the coming weeks, both Grandfather Mountain and Mount Mitchell will provide great vantage points to see fall color in the valleys.)

Mount Mitchell State Park: You'll likely see color change beginning on the approach road up to the summit of Mount Mitchell, the highest peak east of the Mississippi River. Drive or hike to the top for an unrivaled view, and bring a jacket—the weather is often much cooler up here! The summit is primarily evergreen balsam trees, so the fall color tends to be on the approach road and slopes below.

Craggy Gardens: In the the Craggy Gardens area hike to Craggy Pinnacle.

Great news – we've extended an exclusive offer that you can take advantage of! Give work, school or retirement a change of scenery .

Safe & Clean Program

Your peace of mind is our priority. As active members of the hospitality community, we follow thorough cleaning and sanitizing procedures to ensure our Airbnb is safe, welcoming, and comfortable for every guest.

Our dedicated team is trained to maintain a clean, healthy environment while providing the personalized service you’ve come to know and trust. Relax and enjoy your stay knowing your safety is always our focus.

WIFI

We have many guests who work remotely therefore, we keep high speed internet up to date.


Best Boomer Town

Asheville, North Carolina, is a vibrant city where retirees thrive. With mild weather, and stunning views of the Blue Ridge Mountains, it’s no wonder Asheville made the list of “Best Boomer Towns.”

Walkable streets are lined with art deco architecture, charming antique shops, and art galleries. Friendly locals and a strong culture of volunteerism make it easy for retirees to stay active, connected, and engaged with the community.


LOCATION -EASY DRIVABLE CITY
10 MINUTE DRIVE MOST POINTS OF INTEREST IN ASHEVILLE

For a true taste of American history from the 30's, we bet this unique rental gives you a glimpse into the past — without sacrificing any modern comforts.

We wanted to create something for the Urban Explorer!
Introduce you to local life, in addition to the amazing experiences that come with it. The perfect getaway or artist's retreat.

We are within an easy 10 minute drive to most points of interest in Asheville:
Biltmore Estate, River Arts District with 200 artists in 22 buildings, Blue Ridge Parkway, breweries, food and music.

Asheville has a reputation as a mecca for artists and musicians, thanks to its blend of natural beauty and culture.

Your Stay is Just a Click Away

For a limited time, enjoy low rates on our cozy Asheville log cabins. Our mission is simple: to make every guest feel truly welcomed.

Every space is thoughtfully designed for comfort and inspiration—perfect for a relaxing getaway or an extended stay. Discover how effortless and memorable your vacation can be, whether you’re enjoying modern conveniences, classic cabin charm, or a blend of both.

We also offer other Five-Star properties in our family, each crafted to reflect the spirit and beauty of Asheville. We look forward to welcoming you soon!

As summer gives way to Autumn in the Blue Ridge Mountains, we invite you to experience the beauty and the bounty of Asheville's harvest season.

You can choose your own Adventure from eating, breweries, hiking, visiting Blue Ridge Parkway, Biltmore Estate, shopping or live like a local. Soak up every moment and make the most of the season.

You might come for the mountains or the Biltmore Estate and leave wanting to come back for more. Or, too simply enjoy nature in any season.

On Friday evenings, you should visit the Drum Circle where you can drum, dance, or watch a cross-cultural experience from 5 to 8 PM at 67 Patton Ave, Asheville, NC 28801 - Pritchard Park.

A Hidden Gem in East Asheville

Nestled in a quiet log cabin neighborhood, yet just minutes from town, this cozy Asheville retreat offers the perfect balance of tranquility and convenience. Sit on the porch with your favorite beverage, listen to the birds, and let your cares drift away.

Our cabin blends rustic charm with modern comforts. Furniture includes pieces inspired by the historic Omni Grove Park Inn, paired with thoughtful local artwork reflecting Appalachian culture. The living room’s abundant windows and skylight bring the outdoors in, framing trees, songbirds, neighboring cabins, and a serene courtyard.

The bedroom is a haven for rest, featuring a gas stone fireplace, large closet, and a Queen SLEEP NUMBER bed, perfect for customizing each side for the most restful sleep.

Enjoy a full complement of amenities: wireless internet, TV, washer/dryer, all linens and towels, and luxurious 3-in-1 citrus + aloe body wash, shampoo, and hand soap.

In our friendly neighborhood there are many different species of animals such as bears, songbirds, rabbits, raccoons, possums, red foxes, woodchucks, chipmunks, and squirrels.

Staying here affords you the opportunity to be minutes to everything and close enough to recharge, see for yourself why Asheville consistently receives accolades for its quality of life. After all life absolutely looks different at this altitude, 2,133′. Whether you're here for a month or more, you're bound to enjoy the vibe that makes Asheville.

FIND YOUR IDEAL ESCAPE

Asheville is a year-round destination. Experiencing all the best adventures that an outdoor lover enjoys. Asheville has a little bit of everything. Nearly 30 booming breweries that make Asheville a craft beer paradise and won an award titled “Beer City, USA.” For a small city, Asheville has an impressive number of James Beard Foundation award winners. Enjoy a vibrant restaurant scene with delicious, seasonal farm-to-table culinary food to the progressive art, to the bohemian culture, to the bluegrass buskers on Wall Street.

LIMITLESS ADVENTURES

Your options for things to do in Asheville and the Blue Ridge mountains are limitless, no matter your age. Try your hand at exploring the mountains on foot, zip lining, biking, horseback riding, soar above the mountains in a hot air balloon, fly-fishing, yoga, or tackle one of our golf courses. However, you choose to explore Asheville, it all makes for an unforgettable vacation. Let the fun begin now.

Foodie vacations offer a wealth of ways to celebrate the local bounty with micro brews, wines, and Asheville's food culture more delicious, diverse, and sustainable for everyone.

Local restaurant scene is all about taking advantage of the seasonal produce.

Asheville provides celebrity chefs, traditional fare infused with experimental cuisine and an eclectic mix of restaurants and cafes that makes the region one of the Top Food Cities in the country.

Eating Asheville walking food tours are guided by our professional foodies to several of Asheville's best restaurants for a private dining experience.

And we have a Tesla level 2 charger available.

The space

listing_name: Modern Cabin with Hot Tub | Sauna | Creek I Garden
description:
Cabin #28 at Firelight at Shope Creek sits on 22 acres of wooded creek side property in the beautiful Shope Creek area of east Asheville, NC. This rustic, modern home shares the property with 7 other small cabins in the valley below. Newly updated in 2023 with 4 bedrooms, 2.5 baths and a new hot tub. A perfect getaway!!

The space
This recently renovated large four bedroom log cabin is nestled on a south facing slope minutes from Pisgah National Forest access roads and parking lots, where hiking and biking trails are plentiful. Downtown Asheville, the Blue Ridge Parkway and Warren Wilson College are also only 15-20 minutes away. Get the best of both worlds at Shope Creek with a mix of vibrant city attractions and the natural beauty that makes the western NC so famous.

The house is equipped with two wood burning fire places. The newly constructed 900 square foot deck has a covered outdoor dining area and a hot tub where you can enjoy the beautiful mountain views and the unobstructed night sky.

The cabin has four bedrooms and two and a half bathrooms. The large master suite has a king bed, a private full bathroom and walk in closet, wood burning fireplace and a 37 inch flat screen tv with cable. The other three roomy bedrooms are each equipped with queen beds and share a full bathroom with a dual claw foot soaking tub and shower. The kitchen is well equipped with all new appliances, blender, coffee maker, microwave.

Wifi is provided, along with two smart tv's and a phone. This area of Asheville has inconsistent cell phone service, so a phone with long distance service is provided for our guests.

The main floor of the home has an eat in kitchen, living room, dining room and a sitting area perfect for settling in with your favorite book. The stacked washer and dryer is also conveniently located on the main floor. We do not allow pets on the property or in the home.

Guest access
You will have access to your entire private cabin with deck and hot tub. There are also shared communal gardens, a creekside sauna, a chicken coop, and a covered pavilion with outdoor fireplace. There are fire pits and chairs to lounge by the creek. Guests may also meander our private hiking trails.

Other things to note
There are seven additional smaller two person cabins on the property that can be seen in the valley below from the deck. All of our two person cabins (#22, 24, 40, 42, 44, 46, and 48) can be found listed individually for booking on Airbnb.

description:

The space
The house is two stories with hardwood floors and an open floor plan. There are three bedrooms. The master has a king and a full bath. The upstairs bedrooms are spacious. One has a queen and twin size daybed that pulls out to form two side by side twins (or a king depending on how you look at it). The other bedroom has a queen and a high quality futon that folds out to a full. The house most comfortably accommodates 6, but can sleep up to 10. There is one full bath upstairs that the two upstairs rooms share.

Other things to note
We provide all linens including 2 towels sets per person and sheets for the bed. We provide dish soap, dishwasher pods, toilet paper, laundry detergent and some basic cleaning supplies. Our kitchen is stocked similarly to a home kitchen and should have all the cooking gear you need to make meals!

listing_name: Nestled Garden Oasis with Mountain Views Downtown!
description:
Every corner of this home is individually adorned with an eclectic bouquet of antique furnishings and inspiring artwork. Gather around a fire pit in a yard offering mountain views. Recline on a warm sofa in a living room with a charming stone wall.

The space
“Dorothy's place is an absolute gem! It's up in the hills in a quiet neighborhood, but only a quick 5-7 minute drive from downtown and a 2 minute drive from a grocery store. The unit is spacious was incredibly comfortable for us (two plus our pup). The care and effort put into the space is super impressive. There is a queen sized bed, full size fridge and sink, a coffee maker with coffee/cream/sugar, tea, a microwave, and toaster oven. There's also a cute little sitting room with a tv and sofa. Further, the bathroom is a comfortable size with plenty of space for two. Dorothy has a board of recommendations for travelers, along with a map with pins to keep track of where her guests are from. Such sweet details! We were so sad to leave her lovely space. Hope to stay again in the future!” ...previous guest review

WE CLEAN COVID CLEAN, leaving the space fresh and inviting for each and every guest! We now have a GERM GUARDIAN air purifier helping to kill airborne viruses, bacteria and mold spores, amongst other things! I couldn’t be happier. We ventilate the space before, during, and after cleaning. We clean and disinfect all surfaces, paying special attention to frequently touched surfaces. We vacuum and mop the floors. Shower mats are washed between guests. We have always taken pride in the way that we clean and have further enhanced such cleaning because we want you to feel safe to relax and enjoy your stay with us!

Lots of space on this double lot on our small mountain leaves you feeling away from it all even though you're merely minutes from downtown. We are super convenient to grocery stores and tons of restaurants. Very open layout with beautiful archways and a unique rock wall add both charm and class. Super clean and equipped with a shared laundry room and great big bathroom. The kitchenette has everything you could need to feel at home when you're away from home. It is equipped with a full size refrigerator, single burner hot plate, a large toaster oven, microwave and a coffee maker. Hulu streaming is available and will be logged in via Roku and mounted flat screen TV. You are welcome to choose from a variety of other streaming channels to log in with your own personal account. Just don’t forget to log out! What else could you need?! :) This is a very spacious 500 square foot tastefully decorated and well equipped lower level space. Plenty of privacy with a separate entrance and a screen door allows guests to enjoy the fresh air and natural light. Outside space qwith porch swing and lounge seating for guests to lounge the day away. Beautiful fire pit area also available for guest use. Suitable for two adults with a couch available for a child; although the space is not child proof and I don't have separate sleeping arrangements other than the couch :)

This space is one of two rentals on the lower level of my home. Each rental has a private entrance on either side of the house and my entrance is at the front of the house so privacy coming and going is not an issue. If this space does not fulfill your needs please check out my other Airbnb listing :)

Also, I will send instructions when it gets close to your reservation. Please read them carefully to be sure you choose the correct apartment upon arrival.

Guest access
A new lounge recently opened at the bottom of our little mountain! It has a super cool vibe with a good atmosphere, small bites, and a food truck all within a 15 minute walk from our home.

The house is just a 2 minute WALK to historic Helens Bridge!!

“The city drops away quickly as you drive up Beaucatcher Mountain from downtown Asheville. College Street—a main thoroughfare through the heart of downtown Asheville, forming one side of Pack Square next to the haunted Art Deco imminence of Asheville City Hall—suddenly becomes a mountain road as it dizzily traverses the side of the mountain. The road enters a gap in the mountain spanned by a lonely, primeval bridge. You have arrived at Helen’s Bridge.”
Online posting from Southern Spirit Guide

Other things to note
Well-mannered, house-broken dogs are more than welcome but please no rambunctious or aggressive animals. No cats please, due to other guest allergies.

listing_name: The Barn At Mountain Meadows
description:
Luxurious,Magical,Serene,Modern,Organic,Circa 1895

We are proud to offer the Barn at Mountain Meadows for your stay in Asheville.
This luxuriously appointed cabin is located 5 miles from downtown and less than 1 mile from the Blue Ridge Parkway.
Breathtaking eastern views and sunrises at 3500 ft. elevation. Borders Pisgah National Forest.

The space
With a list of features including

•California King size bed.
•4 person hot tub. Gaze at the stars , surrounded by ambient lighting and lush plants ( Drained, Cleaned and refilled after each guest)
•Full kitchen featuring modern appliances , fully stocked with utensils and everything needed for that chef or just someone that loves to cook.
. Out door gas grill & Summertime Organic Herb garden.
•Granite counter tops.
•Eco friendly natural stone and wood construction.
• Reverse osmosis filter system.
•Custom stonework in the shower and kitchen / dining area.
•Hi-speed wireless internet
•Two 42'' TV's with surround sound and fire TV stick streaming (living room and bedroom).
•Washer, Dryer, Dishwasher.
* Plant based- Paraben free- Shampoo-conditioners-shower gels soaps-detergents all provided..
*Ambient controlled lighting in every room
* Fire Pit
•Magnificent views from 3,500 ft.

You will be surrounded by natural wood and stone.
Originally built as a carriage house in 1895, the property has several historic features that have been painstakingly preserved including hand laid unmortared stone walls along the lower section of the western wall and a 120' by 15' unmortared stone wall along the driveway / parking area. With renovations completed in the summer of 2012 including a hand-mortared natural stone shower, The Barn is now a green built, energy efficient home, complete with a reverse osmosis filtration system as well as concrete flooring hand polished with tung-oil.

Relax and take in the views.
The Barn features an eastern view from the mountainside and has a patio with a 4-person hot tub, fire pit, and propane grill. Perfect for taking in the sunrise or relaxing by warm fire on a crisp autumn evening after enjoying the sights of Asheville and the stunning natural beauty of the surrounding area. Located on 4 acres bordering national forest land you will be surrounded by natural beauty and luxury.

Enjoy our fully equipped kitchen
With a full range of modern appliances and plenty of granite counter space to indulge your culinary creativity.

Guest access
Lower floor and outdoor areas. The second floor is only for storage.

Other things to note
Organic Herb and vegetable Garden for your personal use.

In-Cabin Massage services for individuals and Couples.
