listing_name: cozy bedroom in the heart of west asheville
description:
Have your own private sleeping quarters just a stone's throw from everything West Asheville has to offer! You can surprisingly enjoy a quiet walk down my street; however at the end of the street is Haywood-home to numerous cafes, bars, & restaurants! Additionally, you are just over 2 miles from downtown and 10 min from the BlueRidge Prkwy. In 'TheSpace' click on 'more' & read EVERYTHING under Guest Access, & if you have questions or need details, please message me via AirBnB prior to booking.

The space
Your 'home away from home' is a medium-sized dwelling which was built around 1930. Although I am an acupuncturist not an artist, I have been told that my home has a warm, artsy feel to it. Currently, I cannot afford a landscaper so please do not be put off by my somewhat 'wild' front yard. The house is in an excellent location & has an arts-n-crafts style charm, without being extravagant! After clicking on 'more' & reading EVERYTHING under guest access, if you have questions or need more details, please message me via AirBnB prior to booking.

Guest access
In addition to your own private bedroom with DOUBLE bed, closet, chest of drawers, & very small desk, you will have shared access to a full bathroom, full kitchen, living room, & front porch. The bathroom is shared with another Airbnb room when occupied. The bathroom entrance is adjacent to your bedroom but not in it. There is room in the kitchen refrigerator for your perishables. The kitchen has every spice (& interesting & ethnic cookbook) & gadget that you would ever want, & probably some you had not previously discovered. When guests have a longer stay, I sometimes offer to do their laundry if needed. This is because the washer & dryer are located in the cluttered unfinished part of the basement which is off-limits for guest access. There is a small desk which can be designated as a workspace in the bedroom, and if you need a larger desk, the dining room table can be used for this. Although there is NO CABLE, the house has Wi-Fi & there is a TV & DVD player in the living room. My grandmother's antique couch has been reupholstered in burgundy velvet since the photos were taken and you will love it!

During your stay
Communication is the key to any good living environment! Please, if you have any question, complaint, or suggestion, no matter how big or small, please communicate this with me as soon as possible so that it may be resolved. If I am in the basement or not at home, text or call me because I will always have my phone on ANYtime day or night. Open communication is my main house rule! I am fun-loving & easily approachable. I am an acupuncturist & Chinese herbalist and sometimes work at home. I love people (especially meeting new ones!) and equally love time alone. I will give you privacy to do your own thing; however, when needed, I love to share my knowledge & enthusiasm for Asheville and all it has to offer (among other things, the mountains & waterways, the culinary bounty, the live music scene, &the arts). Occasionally, when time permits & invited, I may join you for a meal or a visit.

Other things to note
The bedroom is designated for 1-2 people, & the bed can only sleep 2 people. If you would like to book the second room in this house, look for 'master bedroom in the heart of west asheville.' My home is not child-proofed. I may consider having a baby or young child as a guest if it is discussed prior to my accepting a reservation. Due to increased use of resources, $30 will be added for a third person in this room. Keep in mind, this room is not large by any means, & your twin size air mattress will fit, but it will be a very 'snug' fit. Pets will ONLY be considered under VERY special circumstances, & in this situation, a non-refundable pet cleaning fee will be added, along with a refundable pet damage deposit.

listing_name: Private Cozy Suite - East AVL - Near BRP
description:
Secret little gem in East AVL offers space all to yourself! Private guest suite is located above our detached garage-cozy & clean - ideal for a couple's getaway or a solo retreat. We are located in a small, quiet neighborhood with convenient access (5 miles) to downtown Asheville, 4 miles to the Biltmore Estate and about 1 mile from the Blue Ridge Parkway. Please note: Small coffee/breakfast nook, but no kitchen or cooking appliances. Shared driveway is tight - car back-up cam is handy.

The space
Cozy, renovated guest suite above our detached garage includes queen size bed with memory foam mattress topper, hardwood floors, updated bathroom with a shower and an eat-in counter top/bar area, mini refrigerator and Keurig coffee maker for your convenience. Starbucks coffee, sugar & cream provided, along with bottled water in the fridge. Please note: There are NO cooking appliances (no toaster, no microwave, no hotplates). If you require these, please book a space with a kitchen. If you want to cook or reheat food, our studio will NOT suit your requirements.

The area is heated & cooled with a mini-split unit. Wifi & Roku available. Weather permitting, enjoy the quaint deck in our backyard garden for your morning coffee or evening nightcap. Private parking for one vehicle. Keyless entry lock to private entrance.

Our space has a couple of quirks: Ceiling is just under 8 feet, so please take note of that if you are tall. The back yard parking space is tight, and maneuverability is an issue, especially for larger vehicles.

Guest access
We have plenty of room for 1 car. Very large vehicles (RV's) cannot be accommodated. Please park on the stone parking area in the back yard. (Please do NOT use the parking space to the left of the garage). Please be aware that this space is tight for vehicle maneuvering, so a back-up camera is highly advisable. After parking, you walk up two steps onto the deck and turn left to a stairway up to the private studio door.

Other things to note
Post Hurricane Helene -- Biltmore Estate has re-opened, as well as downtown Asheville. Blue Ridge Parkway has opened selected sections, including East Asheville north to Mount Mitchell State Park. Traffic around town can be a bit heavy due to Hurricane Helene road repairs/closures. Please do your research regarding activities during your visit to make sure you can enjoy your preferred activities and experience AVL safely.

This is a list of some of the things we recommend in Asheville:

Food: Just about any restaurant here is good. Very close to our place we like Copper Crown for great burgers, Filo for coffee/dessert, Dobra Tea for Eastern teas, sweets/savories.
In West Asheville, we like Early Girl and Sunny Point for breakfast. Many downtown restaurants require reservations. For fancier dining, we like Chestnut. Chai Pani makes great Indian street food. Heading east into Swannanoa, Terra Nova is a great stop for beer and a meal. The Pure and Proper in Black Mountain is a fantastic restaurant. We usually do special celebrations there.
Breweries: Highland Brewing in East Asheville is very close to our space.
Hiking nearby: Currently we drive to the Folk Art Center, which is about 5 minutes away from our place. We use the Mountains to Sea trail and stick to the west side of the trail for a fairly easy hike in town. We also like to drive north on the Blue Ridge Parkway. Craggy Gardens is lovely & a great place for a picnic and sunsets. Craggy Pinnacle is also a great hike. A steeper hike would be Rattle Snake Lodge at Bull Gap trailhead on the Parkway. We have not been up there since Hurricane Helene, so use your judgment regarding trails off the Parkway.
Biking: There are several bicycle, e-bike and other fun tours to book around town. Check French Broad Outfitters online.
French Broad River: Please check water quality on French Broad River website. French Broad Outfitters has got you covered if you like the water. Super fun to rent paddle boards or kayaks for a paddle down the French Broad River. Or for a slower vibe, rent a tube and float. There are several other good outfitters in town.
Please let us know if you have any questions!

listing_name: Sparrow Cottage
description:
This bright, cheerful, and clean cottage is a little home away from home! Walking distance to Downtown Asheville, local shopping, restaurants and night life and easy access to the Blue Ridge Parkway and surrounding attractions.

The space
Live like a local! Sparrow Cottage is a clean and cozy one bedroom, one bath house only a half mile from UNC-Asheville, Trader Joe's, Whole Foods local market, Harris Teeter supermarket, a local diner and a coffee shop. It's an easy one-mile walk to the heart of Downtown with its great restaurants, galleries, shopping and nightlife.

The space is inviting, clean and bright! The front door opens out to a great front porch with a sitting area, perfect for a morning cup of coffee or evening glass of wine! The yard is grassed and fenced. The open floor plan includes a living room, dining area and fully equipped kitchen. The full bathroom has a tub and shower combo. The bedroom offers a comfy queen-size bed, closet, chifferobe and nightstands. The loveseat in the living room converts to a full size sleeper with deluxe foam mattress.

The cottage is located on a main street with 35 mph posted speed limit so there is some noise but most guests do not find that a problem. A new fire station a block away is designed so the noise of exiting fire engines is directed away from the cottage, and the engine circles back into the bay with no beeping. There is only one engine at the station and it has not caused a problem for guests or neighbors in the surrounding area. A sound machine is provided. Please read my reviews.

Please email if you have any questions or special circumstances; we do our best to accommodate guests at Sparrow Cottage and provide an excellent stay in our beautiful city!

Guest access
- TV
- WiFi
- Full-size refrigerator
- Toaster
- Blender
- 4-cup coffeemaker and French press
- Oven & Stove
- Microwave
- Basic Kitchen Equipment (Pots & Pans, Plates, Cups, Flatware, Etc)
- Shower/ Bathtub
- Bath Towels
- Hair Dryer
- Bed Linens
- Iron & Ironing Board
- Window Unit AC in Bedroom and Kitchen
- Baseboard heaters
- No gas in the Cottage, so no carbon monoxide detector

Other things to note
Hurricane Helene left a lot of destruction in Western North Carolina. Cleanup and recovery is well under way across the region. Sparrow Cottage and the surrounding area, including downtown, did not flood and much of the physical damage that occurred has been cleaned up. Asheville is open for business and pleasure!

listing_name: Compassionate Expressions Mtn Inn & Spa Retreat
description:
We keep things real and natural but ultra comfortable! This is a private retreat for people who need to getaway from their stressful, busy lives and get centered and refreshed again. We offer a low stimulus environment....letting Mother Nature replace your TV and internet.

The space
Compassionate Expressions Mountain Inn & Healing Sanctuary is more than just mountain lodging, we offer a unique variety of quality and personalized healing spa services. Only 30-minutes northwest of Asheville, located in the still largely secluded mountains of Leicester - you will marvel at the quaint historical farmlands of corn and cattle - where at night you can see a full array of brilliantly lit stars!

Guest access
There are plenty of places to explore on our 40-acre Certified Wildlife Habitat. There are hiking trails and a country road to walk, visit and feed our goats and organic laying hens, enjoy the trickling creek and Meditation Island or our community Fire Pit included in your stay.

description:
Visit but live like a native. 1/2 a block walk to iconic Haywood Rd. Amazingly close to everything but also amazingly private, with private entrance and driveway, solar lights and fire pit. The space
The best thing about this house is its location. Walk anywhere. 1 min walk to historic west Asheville strip. 20 min walk to downtown, 5 min drive, or $7 uber.

Furnished walkout basement with a private entrance, off street parking, bathroom, living room, area to dine and prep meals, and large bedroom with a king tempurpedic bed.

description:
*Pool opens on the 8th of May & Pool closes 9/21.

Welcome to Asheville! Our cedar ranch style home is conveniently located in W Asheville close to all major throughways & interstates. Our home is 1500sqft,3BR,2B,3Q, hardwood, ceramic tile bathrooms, deck, pool,hot tub. Wifi, Pet Fee of $80. No pets are allowed in the pool area at anytime. Pets are to be crated inside the house when away.

NO SMOKING IN THE HOUSE

⭐️with weekend rentals I require that you book both Friday and Saturday together

The space
We like to think that we have a very warm and welcoming home. Hardwood floors throughout and ceramic tile in the bathrooms. We have a quarter wrap around deck on the back of the house and a private swimming pool/hot tub. We provide all modern necessities. The pool opens in mid May and closes around mid September. Hot tub open year round.

Check in-4:30pm unless agreed upon differently.

Check out-10am unless agreed upon differently.

listing_name: Montford Manor (walk to downtown)
description:
This beautiful arts & crafts, green-certified home is located on a quiet dead-end street and conveniently located to the vibrant downtown Asheville area. Montford Manor has all the comforts of home and much more. NOTE: The AirBnB map of our property may be misleading. Montford Manor is NOT located downtown, but a short 25 min walk or a 5 minute drive to Pack Square in the heart of downtown Asheville.

The space
Montford Manor has all the comforts of home and much more. All 4 bedrooms feature Serta I-Comfort adjustable beds (head and foot raise and lower). The master bedroom has a King size bed, all others are Queen size beds. While there are 4 beds to comfortably sleep 8 people, a Queen size sleeper sofa and comfortable Queen inflatable bed are also available for additional guests. All 4 bedrooms have TVs connected to Roku (guests provide their own sign on to apps). There are 3 full bathrooms (one with a tub). The owner's suite has it's own private bathroom. 2 bedrooms upstairs share a bathroom. The bedroom downstairs has a full bathroom nearby.

The kitchen is fully equipped with modern appliances, utensils, cookware and bakeware to prepare home cooked meals. A gas grill is on the outdoor deck, located just outside of the kitchen.

High Speed internet is available via WiFi throughout the house. Relax and enjoy the fresh mountain air on our rocking chairs on the wrap around front porch or on one of the 2 outside decks. Take advantage of our high efficiency washer and dryer to wash your clothes before returning home. There is parking for 2 medium sized vehicles and street parking available for a few cars.

Guest access
You will have access to the entire house (excluding certain storage areas that are locked)

Other things to note
Montford Manor is near Downtown Asheville, just 20 minutes on foot or 5 minutes by car. The Asheville Visitors Center is a short walk away (10 minutes), where you can book anything from tickets to Biltmore House or zip lining and white water rafting, or take a lovely \Hop On/Hop Off\" trolley tour. There are restaurants and coffee shops within walking distance as well. The nearest grocery stores (Harris Teeter and Trader Joes) are just a 5 minute drive away. Montford Manor is located just 20 minutes from the Asheville Regional airport. The home has convenient access to I-26

listing_name: Cozy AVL Suite Near Everything
description:
Sweet private sanctuary only 7 min. to the heart of downtown. Private entrance, fully-loaded kitchenette, super comfy queen memory foam bed and mattress topper.

Recently renovated with full private bathroom, separate living area with couch, A/C & dine-in table.

Fully fenced back yard and hot tub access under large trees. Dog friendly w/ access to disc golf putting & badminton.

Private driveway w/ off-street parking. 5 min to Blue Ridge Parkway, and 5-10 min drive to almost anywhere in town

The space
Sleeps up to three people comfortably (perfect for a small family or a couple plus one friend). Clean and recently remodeled with beautiful cork flooring and two lockable private entrances. Recreational amenities include: badminton court, disc golf basket, hammock, fire pit, hot tub, screened porch, and chicken coop. Fresh towels and linens are waiting for you. Kitchenette includes mini-fridge, microwave, hotplate, sink, toaster, toaster oven, air fryer, cookware, plates, utensils, glassware, pour-over and also french press with organic coffee, tea, and the fixins.
Wi-fi available with booster in the suite. Please note there is no tv.

Guest access
This is a ground-level mother-in-law suite attached to the main house, yet is completely self-contained and private. I live on the other side of the house with my two friendly dogs. You'll get a key to keep your belongings safe and there's a little lock box in the bedroom closet.

Other things to note
PET POLICY: $25 nonrefundable pet fee for any dog you plan to bring. Please let me know in advance of your stay if you intend to bring a dog. If you do not disclose and allow for us to prepare the suite for the dog, it will then be a $50 pet fee. The pet fee can be paid in cash, Venmo or through the air bnb app. I will allow one friendly and well-behaved dog to accompany your trip (so long as it is at least a year old, and that shots and flea/ tick treatments are up to date). Any damage done to the suite or furnishings shall be immediately reported and covered by you. Please no excessive barkers; it drives my dog to the brink of madness which in turn drives me to the brink... thanks in advance for understanding.

COVID CONSIDERATIONS: We take it seriously and expect you to as well. We are using the sanitization and cleaning protocols set forth by the CDC, and even though it’s no longer a requirement, we are still maintaining at least 24 hours between reservations to air the space out. Any linens, comforters, and face masks provided have been cleaned and sanitized between each guest . We will limit our contact with you and wear masks when interacting (unless we can keep at least 10 feet apart while outdoors). If you don’t believe in science and common decency, this is not the spot for you. I want all my guests as well as my family to feel as safe as possible in these times.

Once you book, please read the house manual and check-in procedures; they answer many questions in advance of your stay.

Also:
It is a shared driveway so please make sure to park all the way behind my house (take the fork in the driveway to the right).

If you access the back yard, please keep the privacy gates closed at all times. (My dogs are curious travelers and I'd rather they stay safe at home).

The hot tub is wonderful, and please make sure to turn the jets off when you are finished with your soak.

I don’t provide a tv in the suite. But there is a wifi booster for you to bring your own devices.

There are plenty of clean towels and linens for bathing, lounging and sleeping.

BRAND NEW, spacious & beautifully remodeled this apartment has tons of natural light, its own private entrance & deck.

I look forward to hosting you!

★ This 750 square foot newly remodeled space on the garden level of my home has its own private entrance.

Private outdoor entrance, private bedroom, bath & deck.

listing_name: Short walk to WAVL.
description:
My cozy 2 bedroom sleeps 4 lucky folks. Just a couple of minutes walk to trendy West Asheville and a 5 minute drive to downtown Asheville, your vacation will be filled with activities before you know it!

The space
My house is located right in the heart of West Asheville. A short walk will bring you to the best bars and restaurants this area has to offer. Check out the popular Sunny Point Cafe for breakfast (featured on Good Morning America) and if you're into craft brews you can walk to several local breweries!
If that isn't enough to fill your schedule, check out downtown Asheville. The arts and craft scene downtown is phenomenal and there is always something being celebrated in town. Take a ride on the La Zoom tour. This big purple school bus takes a comedy tour through the different areas of town, highlighting Asheville's extensive history.
For the outdoorsy type, my house is located a short 10 minute drive from the Blue Ridge Parkway and NC Arboretum. Get out and experience all Asheville and the surrounding area has to offer!

Guest access
I want my guests to feel at home while staying at my house. I have two bedrooms: one with a king bed and one with a queen bed. I'll have clean sheets and towels ready when you arrive. I will also have the Wi-Fi password ready so that you can stay connected while on vacation. Though I will have some personal stuff in the fridge, I will make sure to leave plenty of space for your yummy leftovers and farmers' market finds. My kitchen is fully stocked and available for your use (though I doubt you'll need it if you check out all the top-notch restaurants in the area). The carport has space for 2 cars and there is parking on the street for about 2 cars as well. The driveway is fairly steep so you might feel more comfortable parking on the street anyway. I am always available for trip suggestions and questions via email and text during your stay. I will try to accommodate you as best as I can!

listing_name: Happy Haven w/ hottub, Gym, & hammocks in West AVL
description:
Venture only 1/2 mile to Haywood Rd, West Asheville for world renowned food, funky pubs, and unique shopping. Then soak in the hot tub or take an outside shower to rejuvenate. Under 10 minutes to Downtown Asheville, River Arts District, French Broad River trails, Outlet Malls, and the Biltmore Estate.
Come live the Asheville Dream!

Cat lovers only. Cat lives in house and comes and goes as he pleases through a cat door.

The space
This private rental is the main floor of our home, which you will have sole access to during your stay. The bedroom includes a memory foam queen bed with a small sitting area, smart tv, coffee, filtered water, electric fireplace, and has a lock on the door for your security. The extra comfy long couch in front of the wood stove in the living room makes a wonderful sitting area that we are happy to share. There is a full kitchen, a full bathroom, a work out gym, and a dining room that is yours to enjoy while you are our guest. There is also central heat and A/C.

We have a sweet kitty named Moose, that lives here with us, so CAT LOVERS ONLY please. He has a cat door to come and go anywhere as he pleases, thus, there is no stinky litter box. He will definitely be in your space with you, so if you have allergies to cats, this is not the place for you. We also have a strict no animal policy, including service animals, due to Moose's old age and the stress and illness that would cause him.

My husband and I (and occasionally our adult daughters) will be staying in the basement of the home coming and going quietly through a separate entrance. There is a locked door between the 2 floors. listing_name: Log Cabin Near Downtown Asheville
description:
Town and country – best of both worlds. Romantic! Semi-secluded, 5 minutes from town, near Blue Ridge Parkway. Close mountain views from the wrap-around deck. Hot tub for 6. Fire pit in quarter-acre yard, chiminea on deck, gas log fireplace inside. Yard completely fenced & gated with playground. Cable TV, high-speed wireless internet. 1- and 2-night stays are available only if they are hemmed in by other bookings. Please email request for information about shorter stays. Dogs may be acceptable.

The space
Red Gate Cottage is a 1,100 s.f. log and stone cabin with 3 bedrooms – queen bed in master and double bed in second bedroom. Third bedroom flexible – either two extra-long twin beds or one king bed. 2 bathrooms – en suite master bath has deep soaking tub with candle sconces. Vintage cabin faced with stone and cedar on the outside and exposed original log walls on inside. Cathedral ceilings throughout are faced with pine planks. Three fireplaces: big river stone fireplace has a gas log, there's a chiminea on the deck and a fire pit in the yard. A great venue for romantic couples, friends, or family groups to celebrate holidays and special occasions.

Guest access
Guests enjoy the private use of Red Gate Cottage and the quarter-acre fenced and gated yard with hot tub and playground. There is parking for 3 cars or SUV's on the concrete driveway. The house is located on a short county road with unlimited parking alongside the road. The electronic door handle has an entry code.

Other things to note
Red Gate Cottage is available and accessible year round. It has central heat & air conditioning and is well insulated. The driveway is kept cleared in the unlikely event of snow. Snow Melt and snow shovels are on hand, as well as kitty litter for tire traction if you need it. Prices are lower in winter - contact host for special pricing.

listing_name: Mountain Views, Private Retreat, 15 min frm dwntwn
description:
Relax, Restore, Rejuvenate.
Our listing is not just a spare bedroom but a spacious, full-sized lower-level suite, located just a few minutes from downtown Asheville.
We have a secluded, peaceful home on a paved, state-maintained road. You can enjoy 6.5 acres of private outdoor space, along with a steam sauna, hot tub & outdoor shower. We also provide easy access to a large deck offering spectacular views from almost 3000 feet - similar those as seen from the Grove Park Inn - only higher up!

The space
We offer a bit more room to spread out than the average hotel or rental space. This is an extraordinary contemporary style property with a full scale suite with private entrance that includes a queen sized bed, with a roomy closet, a small full bathroom, and sauna The adjoining living room has a sectional couch and pull out queen bed (up-charge for third and fourth person). The living room has high ceilings and similar views as the Grove Park Inn. On a clear day the south facing windows can have panoramic views of 30-40 miles. While we don't offer a kitchenette, we do provide you with a coffee-maker, microwave, a half-size refrigerator

Guest access
Guests have access to the entire lower level suite. Additionally, guests are permitted to use the lower level patio, main level patio with hot tub and outdoor shower, & grounds (6 1/2 acres). There is also a half bath on the main floor that is available when necessary. We also offer private parking.

Other things to note
Our state-maintained road is 1.9 miles from Beaverdam Rd., and it is curvy but an easy drive. The concrete driveway to our home is in a hairpin curve, under a streetlight, and is straight/steeply up. There's a mailbox with our number, and a large stone marker with three 'R's'.

listing_name: West AVL Urban Retreat (Monthly)
description:
This West Asheville home privately located on a quiet residential street, right next to Carrier Park and Asheville Food Park! Haywood Rd (West Asheville's most happening street) and the River Arts District are both a short walk.

The space
This three story house has been recently updated, full of all the amenities you need to make your trip comfortable. Enjoy fast WiFi all throughout the house as well as decks on every level. The lower floor is the master suite, which has a desk with lamp, walk in glass shower, comfortable queen size bed, iPad and TV for Netflix/YouTube. An open kitchen/living area is on the main floor along with a half bathroom. Coffee is provided each morning via a convenient Keurig machine. The top floor has two bedrooms (with spacious closets) and a bathroom. One of the bedrooms has a deck right off of it as a space for docking your laptop to work comfortably.

All TVs throughout the house have a Chromecast connected so you can stream your favorite content from your phone or the iPad.

Each level has independent heating/air control.

Other things to note
We are looking for stays arriving within around a week of the next available dates. Stays longer than 30 days are possible and welcome, just let me know so I can open the dates you need.

The listing price already includes a monthly discount. Stays of 90 days or more do not require sales or occupancy tax in NC and this will already be reflected on the total when booking.

Details on how to access the space and lots of other helpful info are located within the \House Manual\" that you'll receive via email once you book."

listing_name: Mixed Dorm Bunk at Sharky's Youth Hostel
description:
Stay in the heart of West Asheville, one of the city’s most walkable and vibrant neighborhoods.

From our doorstep, you can easily walk to local restaurants, cafés, bakeries, organic markets, pubs, live music, and vintage shops no car needed.

This listing is for one bed in a six-bed, mixed-gender dorm in a friendly, communal hostel that attracts travelers from all over the world and It’s a great fit for solo travelers, backpackers, and social guests who enjoy meeting new people.

The space
This is a shared hostel-style property designed for travelers who enjoy community and shared spaces.

Guests stay in a six-bed co-ed dorm with comfortable beds, and all guests have access to shared amenities throughout the house.

The atmosphere is relaxed and social, with a generally younger crowd (no age restriction).
Fresh linens and towels are provided for all guests.

Guest access
Guests have full access to all shared spaces, including:

Fully equipped shared kitchen

Indoor common rooms

Front and back porches

2.5 shared bathrooms

Self check-in begins at 3:00 PM. Check-in instructions will be sent on the morning of your arrival.

Other things to note
This is a co-ed dorm. We also offer a female-only dorm in a separate listing.

This is a communal hostel environment, not a private accommodation.

Best suited for guests who are comfortable with shared spaces and a social atmosphere.

While there is no age restriction, most guests tend to be on the younger side.

listing_name: Haywood House Asheville (West Asheville)
description:
Haywood House: West Asheville's Newly Restored And Most Walkable Rental! Fully Restored And Updated Craftsman Home With 3 Bedrooms And 2 Full Baths.

The space
The Haywood House is a newly restored, marvelous craftsman home in walking distance to the heart of vibrant West Asheville. Stay in West Asheville’s most walkable rental and enjoy the local restaurants, coffee shops, pubs, ice cream parlors, bakeries and groceries, while leaving the car at home!

It retains many features original to the house such as flooring, fireplaces and mantles, windows, and mouldings, but with all the comforts of home. The easy-access, one-level home has ten-foot ceilings and tastefully decorated throughout with period furniture, antiques, lamps and light fixtures. Haywood House is reminiscent of a time gone by, its craftsman charm updated with all the modern appliances and amenities.

All three-bedrooms boast king size beds. Eight can sleep comfortably including the convenient sleeper sofa in the living room. While the hall bathroom includes a tub and shower, the master bath has a large walk-in shower and ample counter space. You will be spoiled by the generous living and dining room, which includes an antique dining table that seats ten. The large kitchen is updated with granite counter tops, plentiful cabinet storage, and stainless appliances including a dishwasher. Dishes, utensils and small appliances are available, along with basic pantry items for easy meal preparation

A spacious, covered front porch beckons you to sit on the swing and take in daily life in the neighborhood, while the back deck includes a gas grill. Enjoy the thoughtful landscaping and gardens as you plan your adventures or relax for quality time.

Guest access
Keypad entry allows our guests to enter anytime after the 4 pm check in time. This allows for total freedom for our guests.

Other things to note
Legal Vacation Rental

The city of Asheville maintains a strict policy regarding the legality vacation rentals. Haywood House has been approved by the city of Asheville to operate as a vacation rental an is in compliance with all related regulations and requirements, including zoning, safety, and inspections. Some people trying to book a stay in Asheville have had reservations cancelled because the city of Asheville closed down an illegal rental. With Haywood House, you will never have the hassles of re-booking and trying to get a refund for this reason. Haywood House is in compliance with the city of Asheville and will not be shut down.

listing_name: Long Weekend Getaway Minutes from Downtown AVL
description:
This mountain home, in a country setting, is just 5 minutes from downtown. Sip coffee in complete relaxation on the deck with the bustle of downtown right at your fingertips. This eclectic mountain home has everything for a perfect long weekend in Asheville.

The space
This mountain home is nestled in beautiful, wooded scenery just minutes from downtown in a quiet, residential neighborhood.

Guest access
There are four bedrooms and three baths to accommodate 6-8 people very well. The primary bedroom has a king bed. The upstairs guest bedroom has another king bed. Both downstairs bedrooms have queen beds in a fun modern room. I have 2 smart TVs, one in the living room and one in the primary bedroom. My house is decorated with tons a fun artwork and vintage items. The garage and a closet in the basement are reserved for my personal use. I will not be present during your stay, but am just a quick text away. This home is in the mountains which is lovely, but makes for a steep driveway yet is completely accessible with any car.

There is a 3 car maximum. 8 guests maximum.

Other things to note
Please be advised this is a mountain home in a mountain setting. This may not be the perfect home for people with mobility issues. There are approximately 7 steps from the driveway to the walkway and 2 steps from the walkway into the house.

The kitchen, laundry, two bedrooms and baths (including the primary bedroom), dining room and living room are on the main level. There two bedrooms, a bath, and a den with two couches in the basement.

I have a regular drip coffee pot, k pod coffee maker, French press and electric kettle. Filters are provided: please bring coffee and creamer, we have the view!

This home is in a residential neighborhood. Sound travels easily, so I ask that guests be respectful in terms of noise. Thanks in advance!

No smoking, no parties. No bachelorette or bachelor parties, no exceptions.

listing_name: 10 Minutes to Downtown & Biltmore, Next to Parkway
description:
This private family-friendly residence is tucked away on a hill in a quiet residential area just a few minutes south of Asheville. Our peaceful 3 bedroom, 2 bathroom home is ready for your next great family adventure. We’ve made a peaceful place for you to relax! Our home offers all of the amenities you'd expect from a great vacation getaway without the hassles of crowds and hotels. Come and see why Asheville is the best!

The space
Located beside the entrance to the Blue Ridge Parkway and just 10 minutes from Downtown Asheville and the Biltmore Estate, you’ll find you are never too far from adventure and excitement. There’s easy access to I-40, 240, and 26, so getting around town is super convenient.

Make sure to stop by the Folk Art Center just off the Parkway, or head on up to Craggy Pinnacle for a more leisurely parkway experience. If you’re in the mood to shop, you are only 15 minutes from local shops downtown and artisan showrooms in the River Arts District. When it’s time to come back and unwind, you’ll find there’s space for your crowd and their souvenirs.

Stretch out on any of our cozy beds with pristine linens. You have seven to choose from! Our two story home has enough space for everyone to get their “down time” undisturbed. The cozy, more private downstairs bedroom comes with an attached bathroom and offers a bit of peaceful sanctuary. The upstairs bedrooms will sleep a crowd, and the common room with oversized sectional is perfect for a movie night. The 47’ TV and Roku will offer something everyone will love.

Do you love to cook when you travel? You’ll find there’s plenty of space in our full sized kitchen and dining area. Gather with family and friends to enjoy local produce from the Asheville Farmers Market. There is space in the ‘fridge for your local brews. Highland Brewing, Catawba Brewing, New Belgium, and many more breweries are only a 10 minute drive away. If cooking on vacation isn’t your thing, you are just a short trip down the highway from trendy West Asheville eateries like Sunny Point Cafe with the Best Brunch in NC!

With so much going on in Asheville, you’ll want a place where there’s no hassling over details and no fighting over space. Take a breath and relax with us in your new home away from home.

Guest access
We provide guests with the code to the keypad on the front door to have easy access to come and go at your leisure. The guests have access to the whole house with the exception of some closets that are locked with owner storage.

listing_name: Asheville's Family Reunion Spot!
description:
Historic Country Estate in Asheville

The Carolina Jewel stands as the most distinctive vacation rental in the Asheville. The main house exudes history character while offering a casual luxury by other properties. The two houses separated by approximately 100 yards connected by footbridge spanning a creek, where water cascades over moss-covered rocks. The porches, decks, benches, and firepit create an idyllic setting for forging cherished memories.

The space
Built from fieldstones gathered the old-fashioned way (a.k.a. hauling rocks from nearby fields in 1845), the Carolina Jewel blends rustic charm with modern perks. Just off the Great Room, you’ll find a fully stocked kitchen ready for your inner chef and a laundry room if you pack light or get muddy on your mountain adventures.

In 1880, a snazzy Victorian addition was tacked on to make room for a growing family—and in 1999, luxury bathrooms joined the party (don’t worry, the vintage vibes stuck around).

The property stretches over four acres of pure mountain magic, with a bubbly stream, firepit for s’mores and stories, swings, hammocks, decks, porches, and a flat grassy spot that practically begs for cartwheels or lawn games.

You’re just 15 minutes from downtown Asheville (yes, Uber and Lyft know the way), 8 miles from Weaverville, and 5 miles from the Blue Ridge Parkway. Rafting, ziplining, and gem mining? All within 20 minutes. There’s even an apple orchard down the road—and we’re always happy to spill the beans on our favorite restaurants and secret local spots.

Funny thing is, many guests plan big Asheville adventures… then end up sipping coffee on the porch, never wanting to leave. We get it.

Guest access
Guest have access to all parts of both houses.

description:
Close to Haywood Rd. description:
A quiet cabin walking distance to downtown and several grocery stores. Great for creative and adventurous folk looking for something outside the norm.

The space
A private little cottage in the trees, just 3 blocks from city life. Even closer to grocery stores and fresh coffee. Quiet and peaceful.

We are a local couple who built this small house from lost treasures and reclaimed materials. The cabin is about 700 sq ft, comfortably furnished with everything you'll need and nothing more. Queen bed upstairs, walk-in shower, open, clean and airy. There's wood stove and radiant floor for heat, a compost toilet and us close by to help if you are not already familiar with its use. listing_name: Clean & Private Bungalow in W. AVL with Hot Tub
description:
Welcome to our private apartment in hip West Asheville. Please note that detailed cleaning and precautions are taken seriously for your and our safety. This peaceful retreat is in walking distance to historic West Asheville. Downtown Asheville, breweries and the River Arts District are a short drive or bike ride away. The Blue Ridge Parkway and hiking trails are only 20 minutes for the adventurous explorers. Come experience the beauty of Western North Carolina & our home.

The space
The private bungalow apartment was built in 2016 to create a space for travelers to get a feel for the Asheville culture and this unique West Asheville neighborhood. The bnb is easily accessed from the back of the house.
A comfy king bed and futon are available for rest. The television offers cable, Netflix and HBO.

West Asheville is an amazing community, please respect our neighbors and be courteous if you bring your pets. Ourselves and our neighbors have dogs. Outside of the fenced part of our yard, please keep your dogs on a leash. There is a pet fee of $50 per stay that is due upon arrival in the form of electronic payment, cash or check. You assume responsibility for your pet if anything incidents occur during your stay.

A hot tub is available for your enjoyment. No more than 3 adults allowed. Please respect quiet hours of 11:00pm-8:00am. Feel free to reach out if you have any questions about our space or Asheville.

Guest access
The bungalow is in the city, so free street is available. Please be sure to take proper precautions and lock your doors. Once you park your car, use the stone path along the right side of the house. The path leads to a fence, open the gate and walk to the back porch where there are French doors. This is your private entrance to the apartment. There are a private doors for storage in the bnb that are locked. We will reach out if an issue arises to access the spaces with your permission.

Other things to note
Our family of three lives upstairs. You may hear us above, but we do our best to be respectful of our guests in the basement. We have 2 dogs , Hazel and Rahzee. They are great with children, but we like to be around if you introduce them to your dog. If you are traveling with your pet, be sure to review our pet policy in the house rules. If you are traveling with children/baby and need a pack n play or toys for entertainment, we can provide those for you to use free of charge.
Our hot tub will be available for your enjoyment, we ask that you shower before entering and follow the posted rules. It really is so lovely.

We really enjoy hosting and connecting with people from all over the world.
Please don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything or have any questions.

listing_name: Clean and Cozy Country Garden Getaway with Hot Tub
description:
Welcome to the Country Garden Getaway! The perfect nature lovers escape to Asheville. Sit on the porch and enjoy a cup of coffee while listening to the bird songs and an idyllic view of the mountains or enjoy the private hot tub.

Located just 10 miles from downtown, you can experience the tranquility of WNC while being in a convenient location, or go all out nature on this trip with being 5 mins from the famous Blue Ridge Parkway.

We have New Co-Host Management, which up's the game here!

The space
Experience a true Asheville custom built eco home. The perfect place for couples or an individual looking for a getaway to the woods. Step outside and listen to the sounds of bird calls, and quiet nature. Watch the stars from a private hot tub with loved ones.

This quiet garden level apartment is sure to impress, it is the entire lower unit of the house with a private patio. I live above and am often scarce and always stay quiet. Complete with large walk-in closet with shelves and hangers, full kitchen, microwave, toaster oven, and a high-end wooden table and chairs. Washer and dryer for your use located in unit.

If you enjoy nature and would like to catch a break from the city, this is the place for you. With convenient location just minutes from the Blue Ridge Parkway, you can enjoy many of the trails we are famous for, or head over to the Warren Wilson College campus and see the breathtaking trails. If mountain biking interests you, I can tell you of quite a few trails that are close by, and offer bike maintenance if needed during your stay.

Downtown Asheville is a quick 15-20 min drive with many world class restaurants and shops. If needed, Uber is easily obtained at this location.


• King-sized bed with luxurious bedding
• Privacy Glass where needed
• Awesome living room with new leather sofa, loveseat and ottoman.
• There are place settings for 4 if you plan to have company for dinner.
• Fully stocked with the eco-friendly basics (cleaners and detergents, shampoo, cotton balls and swabs, etc.)
• No need to filter or to buy bottled water. The whole-house RainSoft water purification system ensures the finest water from our mountain well for your drinking, washing and bathing needs. Feel free to take some home with you.
• This unit can be sometimes be rented with Sunny Modern Country Apartment, upstairs, which hosts up to 4 guests. ( abnb #7630648 ). Currently, host lives there, which ensures no more noise complaints)

Guest access
I have 2 houses on this property. This home has separate AIRBNB units upstairs, and downstairs, that are separated by locking doors and a stairwell. (You can book the whole house.)

Guests have full access to the front yard (.5 acre), which has a nice little frog pond, and is good for frisbee, bocce, or just to stretch your legs.

• Please respect private areas. (See map in pictures on listing and in manual.)
• The old wooden barn is the Cantina, a social bbq spot for all guests.
• Children should be supervised at all times. Keep children out of barn.
• Enjoy the garden level patio with 2 waterproof areas, and the new side garden area next to the spa.
• Hot tub chemistry is delicate. Guests must shower before entering, and if you wear a suit, it must be freshly washed and rinsed to avoid tub contamination.
• Any abuse of the hot tub may result in damage deposit loss

Other things to note
• Shope Rd. is a private gravel road and does not require 4wd. It does not get plowed in winter, and snow is rare. Full-length RV's will not fit up the driveway.
• Verizon is the only reliable cell network in the Riceville area, and throughout most of WNC.
• From your patio, you have a wonderful view of our neighbor’s 1890’s era farm with cows and goats, beneath Peach Knob and the Blue Ridge Parkway.
• To ensure your privacy, we provide full blinds in the bedroom area, and privacy glass in bathroom.

Sam's Cottage is the reincarnation of an what may have been a Mill, later an barn, then an Appalachian home. The building has been completely renovated for modern convenience and style, but the original pine floors and wormy chestnut paneling were salvaged to grace the home.

listing_name: THE SWEET SPOT- serene hilltop, close to downtown
description:
Serene hilltop residence with panoramic view of the Blue Ridge mountains and a remarkable view of the Biltmore estate. Well stocked kitchen for entertaining or celebrating a special life event or a perfect place for quiet reflection in a romantic setting.
*Drive 10 minutes or < to -
Downtown shopping, theater
Art district
Biltmore
NC Arboretum
Zen Tubing - French Broad River
Lake Powhatan - swimming
Blue Ridge Parkway
Fly fishing
*No children between the ages of 5 mos. and 7 yrs. No pets.

The space
The Asheville area welcomes you back after Hurricane Helene. Check out the Explore Asheville website for a detailed list of activities and attractions that are open now!

Newly constructed, pristine residence. Parking on premises. Two steps up to a small front porch, welcomingly decorated. Front entrance with easy code access. Picture windows upon entry into the main area with striking views of the Blue Ridge Mountains and a remarkable view of the Biltmore castle. Well stocked kitchen with spices and baking needs, snacks, Keurig coffee maker with (selection of) pods and cream to start you off. Sizeable counter space which doubles as a breakfast nook and contains a basket which holds a bottle of Biltmore wine and a couple of local snacks, a gift for guests. Also on this counter is a well-organized, informative folder consisting of suggestions for things to do, places to eat and to go. Ice maker, convection oven and dishwasher. The dining area, seats six, is adjacent to a picture window with a lovely view. A romantic arena: table adorned with copper candle stick holders and place settings elegantly decorated with chargers. The living room also has picture window view and is great for socializing or relaxing, has a fireplace which gives it a cozy feel and a quality sound system. A shelving unit sits adjacent to couch with cabinets which are stocked with items of entertainment; games, magazines, books (including guidebooks) and things of interest related to Asheville. There are two outdoor porches, one partially enclosed and private off the living room. This is set with a couch and chairs, coffee table with a drawer of games. It has a ceiling fan for those hot days and is adroitly decorated with plants and flowers (weather permitting). Another open porch off the dining room holds a patio set and Char-Broil grill, great area for morning coffee. The master bedroom with bath has a walk-in closet and six drawer dresser, a smart TV with high-speed internet and streaming capability. Also an Armoire which is amply stocked with extra linens. The full bed is set with soft, signature sheets and comfortable blankets and there is a phone/watch charger on bedside table. Walk in closet contains weights and yoga mat for those who are interested in keeping a fitness routine and a small fan if needed for background noise. A small cooler is also stored in this closet as is an ironing board and iron. The bath is stocked with toothpaste, floss, nail files and body lotion and a soap which smells delicious. Body wash, shampoo and conditioner are also provided. There are his and her Turkish robes for guest use on back of bathroom door. Second bedroom/office, with adjacent bathroom, holds a desk top Mac computer and printer. Also signature soft linens on a full Murphy bed, and a phone/watch charger. There is a closet and a dresser with six drawers. Guest bath is stocked in same fashion as Master and also has a unisex Turkish robe. Washer and dryer are tucked away in a closet adjacent to Master. Centrally located with easy access to I-40 and I-26 and is ~ ten minutes from downtown Asheville, a haven for foodies and the Asheville Airport.
*Sorry, but children between the ages of 5 months and 7 years are not permitted, as this dwelling is not child proof. No pets allowed.
Thank you for your interest!

Guest access
Guests have their own entrance, and have sole access to a two bedroom, two bath, three porch dwelling. First floor is occupied by myself (Terry) and I have a separate, back door, entrance.

Other things to note
Couple of unique things about my place are -
When the sun is shining (which averages to about 212 days per year) there is an unobstructed view of the Biltmore Estate from both porches. Whenever they have a fireworks display, you have a ringside seat : )
View of the Blue Ridge Mountains is stunning!
The Farmers Market, which is 3 miles away (570 Brevard Road), is open daily throughout the year and boasts a bountiful selection of local fruits and vegetables as well as meats and cheeses. It’s an Asheville treasure!

listing_name: Viewy West Side Garden Apartment
description:
This basement level suite is a clean, new-built space with lots of natural light, privacy and views of our urban homestead and the mountains beyond. It is the perfect spot from which to explore Asheville and the surrounding area. Located just 2 miles from downtown, it is an easy walk to the French Broad River as well as the many restaurants and shops of West Asheville. This space is great for couples, families, solo adventurers, and business travelers.

The space
There is a private bedroom with a queen size bed and one full bath. The space has a large living space with a TV and lots of games and books. There is a small table for sipping coffee in the morning or to use as a work station.

For visitors with children, there are loads of board games and books that can be enjoyed during your stay as well as a collection of toys that can be pulled from the bedroom closet.

The private bedroom has a queen size bed and there are two twin sized IKEA bed mats that can be made up on the floor in the living space for sleeping 4 people total. All sheets, blankets and towels are provided.

Guest access
Guests are welcome to ample, free street parking. The basement suite is on a separate level than our living space, is easy to access and has a private entrance.

Guests are welcome to use the backyard for daytime lounging, exploring the gardens, and playing in the big yard (especially nice for children).

Other things to note
We are a busy, active family. On weekdays, typical getting-ready-for-school/work noises will occur, but we leave a quiet house behind and are most often gone for the bulk of the day. On weekends we typically sleep later and are busy with sports and adventures throughout the days.

This listing is permitted and in full compliance with local homestay regulations (permit number 21-02015).

listing_name: Spacious bedroom in the heart of West Asheville
description:
Have your own private sleeping quarters just a stone's throw from everything West Asheville has to offer! You can surprisingly enjoy a quiet walk down my street; however at the end of the street is Haywood-home to numerous cafes, bars, & restaurants! Additionally, you are 2miles from downtown & 10 minutes from the Blueridge Prkwy. Under 'The Space' click on 'more' & read EVERYTHING under Guest Access, & if you have questions or need more details, please message me via AirBnB prior to booking.

The space
Your 'home away from home' is a medium-sized dwelling which was built around 1930. Although I am an acupuncturist not an artist, I have been told that my home has a warm, artsy feel to it. Currently, I cannot afford a landscaper so please do not be put off by my somewhat 'wild' front yard. The house is in an excellent location & has an arts-n-crafts stye charm, without being extravagant! After clicking on 'more' & reading everything under guest access, if you have questions or need more details, please message me via AirBnB prior to booking.

Guest access
In addition to your own private bedroom with QUEEN-size bed, closet, dresser, & large desk, you will have SHARED access to a full bathroom, full kitchen, living room, & front porch. The bathroom is shared with another Airbnb room when occupied. The bathroom entrance is directly across the hall from your bedroom, but not in it. There is room in the refrigerator for your perishables. The kitchen has every spice (& interesting & ethnic cookbook) & gadget that you would ever want, & probably some you had not previously discovered. There large desk in the bedroom which can be used as a designated workspace. For guests with longer stays, I sometimes offer to do their laundry. This is because the washer & dryer are located in the cluttered unfinished part of the basement, which is off-limits for guest access. Although there is NO CABLE, the house has Wi-Fi & there is a TV & DVD player in the living room. My grandmother's couch has been reupholstered in burgundy velvet since the photos were taken, and you will love it!

During your stay
Communication is the key to any good living environment! Please, if you have any question, complaint, or suggestion, no matter how big or small, please communicate this with me as soon as possible so that it may be resolved. If I am in the basement or not at home, text or call me because I will always have my phone on ANYtime day or night. Open communication is my main house rule! I am fun-loving & easily approachable. I am an acupuncturist & Chinese herbalist and sometimes work at home. I love people (especially meeting new ones!) and equally love time alone. I will give you privacy to do your own thing; however, when needed, I love to share my knowledge & enthusiasm for Asheville and all it has to offer (among other things, the mountains & waterways, the culinary bounty, the live music scene, &the arts). Occasionally, when time permits & invited, I may join you for a meal or a visit.

Other things to note
The bedroom is designated for 1-2 people, and has a queen size bed. If you would like to book the second room in this house, look for my other listing 'cozy bedroom in the heart of west asheville', which has a double or standard size bed. My home is not currently child-proofed. I may consider having a baby or young child as a guest if it is discussed prior to my accepting a reservation. Due to increased use of resources, it will be $30 to have a third person share this room. A twin size air mattress will fit, but the room is, of course, more comfortable without it. Pets will ONLY be considered under VERY special circumstances, such as a seeing- eye do, & in this situation, a non-refundable pet cleaning fee will be ADDED, along with a refundable pet damage deposit.

listing_name: Unicorn Farm's modern living in a country setting
description:
(We are back! Please see Storm Recovery Note in property description)
Our studio apartment is located in a country setting just 20 minutes from the city center. Its walking distance to Pisgah National Forest with its hiking & mountain biking trails. We are also close to the Biltmore House, Nature Center, Blue Ridge Parkway and Folk Art Center. The property is bordered by a mountain stream, and pastures. Our guests love the quiet cozy feel, beautiful views, and convenient location.

The space
Storm Recovery Note: We did not sustain any damage to the apartment however we did sustain considerable damage to our property and landscape. We have made significant progress in restoring much of our property but still have work to do. The apartment and landscaping immediately around the apartment is still the same beautiful quality our guests have come to expect so we hope you will return and help support us as we not only recover the rest of the property but make it even better than ever.

Local area note: The hiking trails that are within walking distance have been temporarily closed while the forest service logs the storm debris. There are several other hiking trails within a very short drive that are still open and very beautiful. When the trails reopen we will remove this part of the note. While area recovery is still in process cleanup is pretty much complete and 95+ percent of restaurants, businesses and roads have reopened.

Property Access note: The directions to our property are still correct however the bridge on Unicorn Farm Road is still out and awaiting repair. You can access our property by taking the new culvert crossing that is to the left after the second bridge on Shope Creek and before Unicorn Farm Road. That will bring you up past our greenhouse operation and to the \green\" house at the top of the property where the apartment is. We will also include these instructions in your reservation messages.

listing_name: A Step Back in Time - 1901 Estate Guest Cottage
description:
Take a step back in time in our cozy Historic Estate's Guest Cottage. The cottage is 400 SF, 100 years old, w/ a rustic charm & peaceful surroundings. FREE PARKING & FREE WASH AND FOLD LAUNDRY SERVICE. We are within close proximity to major attractions like The Biltmore House & Downtown Asheville. Extremely comfortable queen bed, fully-equipped KITCHENETTE, closet space. Good for couples, girls trips, solo & business travelers!

The space
Everything about the guest cottage is super cute. It’s IDEAL for 1 or 2 guests MAX and may not be ideal for folks 6’4 or taller.
Our home sits on 2.5 acres in an historic neighborhood a quarter way up one of the smaller mountains in South Asheville.
The cottage is rustic in nature and constructed entirely of wood. It was MODIFIED at some point with modern-day conveniences like a KITCHENETTE, has a small, full bathroom, solar windows, and ample closet space. Jim and I did quite a bit when we moved here to give it a cozy and comfortable homey feel.

The kitchenette is well-equipped (no oven) but all necessary kitchen items including dishes, utensils, wine opener, microwave, toaster, coffee maker, ice maker and hot plate.
High Speed Internet/WIFI, parking for up to 2 guest cars, complimentary coffee, creamer sugar, popcorn. No need to bring bottled water, we have BOTH an UNDER-SINK WATER FILTER AND A BRITA WATER FILTER PITCHER.

The cottage has antique furnishings and very comfortable queen bed and Murphy Bed is an XL Tempurpedic mattress. The bathroom has a full seated shower and adjustable shower head.

We offer FREE wash and fold service if needed. DETAILS UPON REQUEST.

Guest access
PLEASE READ FULLY: You'll have your own parking space. There are 3 PATHS TO THE COTTAGE. One STONE STEP path in our backyard with a handrail, a gravel driveway (NO STEPS) around the back of the cottage, and one path to the side of the cottage (SOME STEPS). THE COTTAGE IS ON A HILL SINCE ASHEVILLE IS A MOUNTAINOUS AREA.
PLEASE NOTE: The grounds have approx 15 degree incline, SO IT IS NOT SUITABLE for wheelchairs, a challenge for crutches, foot/ankle injury boots, or knee/hip issues and older adults. The historic nature of the cottage makes it unsuitable for wheelchairs since the doorways aren't wide enough and the bathroom isn't large enough to fit a wheel chair.

Other things to note
For Guests arriving at dark: This neighborhood is VERY DARK at night, as there aren't many street lights. But the cottage has motion lights that come on for the STONE PATH up to the cottage, and we keep the front porch light on.

Guest access

Guests should know that we have a separate personal space in the lower level where we occasionally stay. We will keep you up to date about when we will be in our space during your stay.

Other things to note
We have games, cards and puzzles.

Parents should be aware that there is a small water feature that is easily accessible in the back yard and young children will need to be closely supervised when they are outside.
Also, if you choose to use the fireplace or gas fireplace available here, children will also need to be closely supervised. These items are optional and would not need to be used to heat the house.

listing_name: Happy Hippie House: Walk to Restaurants/Shops/Bars
description:
Totally groovy!

Great, West Asheville location that is walkable to shops, restaurants, and bars! A 7-minute drive to downtown.

The space
You’re gonna totally dig this groovy getaway called the Happy Hippie House. It’s got the best location in all of Asheville since it’s in a cute residential neighborhood that’s a quick walk to West Asheville’s popular bars, breweries, restaurants, and shops – including Instant Karma, the coolest hippie shop east of the Mississippi! It’s also a quick 7-minute drive to downtown, 10 minutes to the Biltmore Estate, and 14 minutes to the Blue Ridge Parkway. So, it’s far out, without being far out! Can you dig it? Plus, you can’t beat the fun, eclectic vibe. It’s right on, man.

Your space is a private apartment is in the garden level of a charming and quirky 1920s home in a quiet residential neighborhood in West Asheville, walking distance to shops, restaurants, and nightlife. (See exact walking times below.) At around 380 square feet, the apartment is spacious enough for a couple of people on vacation, including a 150 square foot bedroom, a 193 square foot open concept living room and kitchenette, and a 38 square foot bathroom.

LIVING AREA - Your private apartment has its own entrance around the back of our home. The apartment includes an open concept living area with a seating area and kitchenette with a kickin’ vibe. The seating area has a smart TV so you can easily view a variety of television channels or log in to your personal Netflix or other streaming TV accounts for when you want to chill with the groove tube. Of course, with the color-changing floor lamp and three uber-cool Lava Lamps, you may find you’ve got other things to chill and watch than a screen.

Free Wi-Fi is also included.

KITCHENETTE - The attached kitchenette has a sink, mini fridge, microwave, and coffee maker. We supply coffee, tea, and sugar.

BEDROOM - The separate bedroom includes a queen-sized bed. The mattress is a firm Serta mattress, and it has a pillow topper added to make it even cozier. On each side of the bed is a bedside table, equipped with a reading lamp and power outlet for cell phone charging. There is a closet with hangers. Inside the closet, we keep an extra blanket, pillows, a fan, an iron, and an ironing board.

Plus, you can’t beat the trippy blacklights for the ultimate dreamy night. You can enjoy the Happy Hippie mural painted with neon-style, UV-reactive fluorescent paint, and lots of classic hippie blacklight posters. There's a track of blacklights all around the ceiling and two blacklight spotlights on the floor. There are also colored fairy lights above the bed.

BATHROOM - The private bathroom includes a sink, mirror, toilet, and shower. We provide towels, shampoo, conditioner, body wash, lotion, and a hair dryer. Oh, and plenty of hot water, dude, so you can enjoy being a clean hippie.

ELECTRIC VEHICLE CHARGER - For those of you Mother Earth lovin’ electric car drivers, we have a 240 V Level 2 ChargePoint charger using J1772 plug (which fits most EVs). Tesla drivers may bring their J1772-to-Tesla adapter.

SOUND LEVEL - We live upstairs, and this old house has creaky floors. Even though we generally try to be quiet, you will hear our footsteps and our dogs at times. We provide a white noise machine and ear plugs, but if you are sensitive to sound, you may prefer to book elsewhere. Other than that, it's all copacetic.

SAFETY - The apartment is a legal, licensed, short term rental that has passed inspection by the City of Asheville for safety and compliance (Homestay Permit 16-12139). It has appropriate egress, smoke detectors, a carbon monoxide detector, fire extinguisher, first aid kit, and an emergency lantern, ‘cause you know, safety first, man.

SERVICE - We are striving to provide a five-star experience for all our guests, so please let us know any suggestions or desires you may have. What would be groovier?

description:

Walk to all the best restaurants, shops, art galleries, music and pop up markets. Biltmore, Grove Park and the Blue Ridge Parkway are just a short drive away.

The space
Our guest house is an 800 sq. ft. addition built onto a historic house that is used as an office right in the heart of downtown. The house is completely separate. This is a 2 story space and the bathroom and bedrooms are all on the second floor (approximately 12 stairs). The master bedroom has a King bed and balcony. listing_name: Peaceful Asheville Sanctuary | 5 Mins to Downtown
description:
Your Asheville adventure begins here! Relax in a peaceful wooded retreat just minutes from downtown’s best restaurants, cafes, and nightlife, and only a short drive to the Blue Ridge Parkway. Ideal for couples, solo travelers, and business guests looking for a serene, dog-friendly central home base after days full of discovery.

While our area experienced Helene's impact, we’re grateful that this peaceful space remains safe, welcoming, and ready for your stay.

The space
I've deliberately kept this space bright, clean, and simple, with basic amenities such as tea and coffee service, a mini fridge and microwave, and dishes. There is no formal kitchen, and no stove or big sink. I also provide basic toiletries in case you forget your own. There ARE curtains on the windows--they were installed after the photos were taken. Dog bowls available, but please bring your own dog beds/blankets.

Games, puzzles, books, & playing cards are available for non-screen time. Should you need to use the internet, wireless is available. Note that THERE IS NO TV. Most of our guests enjoy exploring our vibrant town, our surrounding natural areas, and the quiet and simplicity of just disconnecting from the world to reconnect to one another.

Please come and go quietly and clean up after yourself. I ask that guests take shoes off upon arrival.

ALL ARE WELCOME HERE.

Guest access
You can access the space directly through two separate private entrances. The spaces you see in the pictures are all yours, exclusively, to enjoy—there is no crossover or sharing with our space. We live upstairs from this space, and there is no overlap.

Other things to note
I have a long-term tenant in the apartment adjacent to the AirBnB. Though her entrance is on the opposite side of the house, you may notice her coming & going or walking her sweet old dog.

While we aren't very noisy, we do create the sounds of footsteps, laughter, and daily conversation. We try our best to keep our voices down, as we live in the upstairs level of the house.

We do not practice discrimination here, and we expect respectful guests.

listing_name: The Snook Nook ~ A Mountain Retreat In Asheville
description:
The Snook Nook is perfectly located just 3 miles from the Blue Ridge Parkway and 8 miles from downtown Asheville. Just hop on the Parkway and have an Appalachian adventure or visit the hottest town the South has to offer as it slowly opens its doors.

One important thing to note - we live above the Snook Nook with our two kiddos and two pups. We do our best to keep the noise level down when guests are here, but you will probably hear some footsteps above.

The space
This is brand new construction as of Fall of 2018. Having hosted the Snook Nook for over a year in our downstairs apartment, we finished off half of our lower level specifically with Airbnb in mind. We still have the old hits in a fridge, a Keurig (with pods), a microwave, a Roku with streaming service and free Netflix, and a queen sized bed. To that, we've added lots more space, a sofa bed in the living space that sleeps one comfortably, a dishwasher, sink, and hot plate (along with pots, pans, and dish ware) in the kitchen, and a Snook Nook Book Nook in the bedroom with it's own Nook-sized library. Folks love the new Snook Nook look!

Guest access
The Snook Nook takes up half of the ground floor of our home. It has its own private entrance with a key code lock. The living space, kitchen, bedroom, and full bath are exclusively yours for your stay. You also have full use of the community garden just steps down the driveway.

Other things to note
During the spring, summer, and fall, we have a community garden that is available to all the properties in Snookville. It includes two picnic tables, a climbing tower, and a sandbox (those last two are our daughter's, but Matilda loves to share.)

We have a super friendly golden mix named Cash and pure golden named Belle--you may see them running around the grounds.

During the winter months, we do recommend four wheel drive.

For more information about the other properties and recommendations, visit snookvillerentals dot com.

listing_name: Historic Church Suite in The River Arts District
description:
The Ora Street Church of God, located between The River Arts District, The South Slope Brewery District, and only 1 mile from Biltmore Estate was built in the 1920s and thrived for over 80 years before being converted into our home. Enjoy a short walk to restaurants, breweries, art galleries greenways and music. This space was formerly the pastor's study and is now your personal master suite. We invite you to stay in our church house, one of the most unique and charming Airbnbs in Asheville.

The space
Our guest are given access thru the more private back door which allows direct access to the stairwell leading up to the master suite. The back door is accessed via our remodeled back deck where you will find a stone fireplace and large TV and hot tub for guests to use. This outdoor space is kept under video surveillance for our safety and for that of our guests as well. This entryway will open into a stairwell where guests will be met with a private minibar with espresso, cocktails and beer for their enjoyment. The stairs from this area will lead you to our remodeled master suite which once operated as the pastor's study. Here our guests will find a luxurious queen bed with his and hers LED reading lamps, mini-fridge, stocked coffee cart, seating area and a private bath. There is a large flat screen tv in the room which you can use for viewing your favorite streaming platform.

Guest access
In addition to the upstairs master suite, guests are given access to the remodeled back deck with fireplace and outdoor seating and hot tub area as a common space (We also use the outdoor space on occasion bur rarely when we have renters). Guests will also have access to the stairwell leading up to their suite which contains a private minibar, espresso and cocktail station as well as a stocked fridge with local craft beer selections. Parking is available on the street in front of or adjacent to our home.

During your stay
My wife and I are extremely social people but recognize that not everyone is that way. We look forward to the opportunity to meet and socialize with guests but only if that is something they are interested in. I am available at all times to provide needed support in order to make for an enjoyable stay. I have an extensive knowledge of the restaurant and brewery scene in Asheville and absolutely love sharing that information with others should they want suggestions or input.

Other things to note
Our space does have a number of stairs so it may be difficult to navigate if you have a disability or an injury or simply have trouble with stairs.
Our dogs Arthur and Amos are the stars of our Airbnb. They love hanging out with guests on the back deck and will be as friendly as you allow them to be.

listing_name: Sandy's Place
description:
Sandy’s Place is conveniently located in the historic Kenilworth neighborhood of Asheville, less than 5 minutes from downtown, the Biltmore Estate, and the River Arts District. Asheville is known for its award winning restaurants, coffee shops, art galleries, music venues, theatre, craft breweries and abundant outdoor recreational opportunities. Our space is the perfect home base for couples, families, and business travelers looking to explore all that Asheville has to offer.

The space
Sandy's Place is in the quiet and friendly neighborhood of Kenilworth. Our home was built in 1939, rich with charm and character. In 1995 we constructed an apartment on the lower level of our three-story home where my mother, Sandy, lived for twenty years. In honor of Sandy and her amazing gift of grace and hospitality, our Airbnb is called “Sandy’s Place.” Look out the windows into a wooded backdrop. There is a sunny deck to enjoy your morning coffee or an evening beverage at the end of your day. The kitchen is a lovely sun-filled space that has many amenities (filtered water, a full-size refrigerator, microwave, toaster oven, and portable induction cooktop) to prepare a light meal, or enjoy takeout from one our nearby restaurants. We are an ecofriendly Airbnb with a dual flush toilet, tankless water heater, cloth napkins, earth friendly non toxic cleaning products, and recycling.

The apartment features two large adjoining rooms, each with a queen sized bed and comfortable seating area. The sunroom is accessed by passing through the first room and is separated by wood and glass folding doors and curtains.

We are a legal, permitted homestay in the city of Asheville.

Guest access
You will be given a code to access your own private entrance at the garden level of our home. Please note there are steps and a ramp down to the apartment. There is parking for two cars in the driveway and more on the street.

Other things to note
Sandy's Place only sleeps four. Guests sometimes ask if a fifth can be accommodated. The couch is not available for sleeping. Any other arrangements need to be discussed with hosts. There is an additional charge for an extra guest of $75 per night per person through Airbnb.

We are happy to ship any items you may have left behind. There will be a $10 fee in addition to the shipping charge.

listing_name: Private Suite: Walk to Biltmore and Breweries
description:
Post-Helene Update: If you are visiting Asheville and all that our beautiful city and WNC have to offer, we look forward to having you. Our local economy (ourselves included) is excited to have you back as we rebuild.

The space
This dog-friendly suite is self-contained and accessible only by its own private entrance. The space is ideal for the lone traveler or couples seeking a romantic retreat in the mountains. The suite includes a spacious bedroom (with queen) and full bath. In addition, our guests can enjoy mountain views from the comfort of the adjoining living space. Guests (and pups) can enjoy the mountain air from the spacious deck or the yard in the back! Pups can run free in the fully fenced private dog park or enjoy strolls with their people through the garden.

Plan your day hikes with our selection of WNC hiking guide books while enjoying your cup of coffee on the porch. In the evenings, sit in the garden with your favorite beverage in hand. Stop and smell the abundance of perennial flowers and enjoy the sun set and the summertime fireflies. You will often find us enjoying the same landscape or keeping the weeds at bay.

Guest access
Guests will have access to a private suite but not the entire house. The suite includes a bedroom, bathroom and an extra room with a desk and chair, small couch, coffee maker, mini-fridge, and microwave. Guests will have access through a private entrance, which requires climbing one flight of stairs.

We are a family of three, including our 5 year old son. You may hear us from time to time, but we have done a great deal of sound proofing between our areas. There is a large pantry between the suite and our living space that we do not use extensively, providing a large sound barrier between us. This also gives our guests peace of mind when traveling with pets. Know that barking doesn't bother us, and we rarely hear it anyways!
